The viewpoint of the relief across the Uda River is registered as a Nara Prefecture Scenic Landscape Asset.
deepdive.legends_jp: ①役行者開基伝承（白鳳9年/681年）：史料的根拠は弱く創建を修験道の祖に仮託した伝承。②空海（弘法大師）建立伝承（天長元年/824年）：同様に創建伝承の一つで史料的裏付けは薄い。③身代わり地蔵：無実の娘が火あぶりの刑に処されそうになった際・地蔵菩薩が身代わりとなって救ったという伝説。
deepdive.legends_en: 1. En-no-Gyoja founding legend (681 CE): historically unverifiable; a common trope attributing temple origins to the Shugendo patriarch. 2. Kukai construction legend (824 CE): similarly a pious attribution without documentary support. 3. Migawari Jizo (Substitute Jizo) legend: a Jizo Bosatsu is said to have substituted itself for an innocent girl condemned to death by burning, thus saving her life.
deepdive.access_jp: 近鉄大阪線・室生口大野駅から徒歩約5分。車の場合は室生口大野駅前の県道28号を室生方面へ約0.5km。大野寺専用駐車場あり（無料）。名阪国道・針ICより約25分。
deepdive.access_en: Approximately 5 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Muro-guchi-Ono Station. By car, 500m northeast along Route 28 from the station; free dedicated parking is available. From Hari IC (Meihan Expressway), approximately 25 minutes.
deepdive.photo_points_jp: 磨崖仏は線刻のため光線条件が見え方を大きく左右する。①最良条件：晴天の午前10時〜14時ごろ・南東向きの岩壁に陽光が正面から当たる時間帯。②雨天・曇天では線刻が見えにくくなる。③冬季（11〜2月）は陽光の入射角が低く像の一部に影が落ちることがある。④春（3月下旬〜4月上旬）は枝垂桜と磨崖仏の競演が人気撮影ポイント。宇陀川の対岸（寺の前）から撮影。望遠レンズ（200mm以上）を推奨。
deepdive.photo_points_en: The incised relief depends heavily on light angle for visibility. 1. Optimal: clear days between approximately 10:00 and 14:00, when direct sunlight strikes the southeast-facing cliff. 2. Overcast or rainy conditions make the shallow incision almost invisible. 3. In winter (November–February), the lower sun angle can shadow parts of the figure. 4. Late March to early April: the cascading cherry tree and the cliff image make a popular combined shot. All photography is done from the temple forecourt across the river; a telephoto lens (200mm+) is strongly recommended.
deepdive.nearby_jp: 室生龍穴神社・吉祥龍穴（車で15分）・室生寺（車で15分）・龍鎮渓谷（車で10分）・近鉄室生口大野駅周辺。
deepdive.nearby_en: Muro Ryuketsu Shrine and Kissho Ryuketsu (15 minutes by car); Muro-ji Temple (15 minutes by car); Ryuchin Valley (10 minutes by car); Kintetsu Muro-guchi-Ono Station area.
deepdive.warnings_jp: 磨崖仏は宇陀川の対岸の断崖にあるため川には近づかないこと。増水時は岸辺が危険。宇陀川沿いの道路は豪雨後に通行止めになる場合がある。
deepdive.warnings_en: The relief is carved into a cliff on the opposite bank of the Uda River — do not approach the riverbank, especially during or after heavy rain when flooding and unstable banks are hazardous. The riverside road may be closed after major rainfall events.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 役行者開基（681年）および空海建立（824年）はいずれも「不確かな情報」であり史料的根拠が確認されていない伝承。見学の際は磨崖仏が線刻（浮彫りではなく陰刻に近い）であり光線条件によっては肉眼でほぼ見えない場合があることを事前に認識すること。

summary_en: On the rock face of the opposite bank of the Uda River stands a Maitreya cliff carving (National Historic Site) 11.5 metres in height. The image was commissioned by the Kofuku-ji monk Gaen in the first year of Jogen (1207), executed by the workshop of Yi Xinmo (Igyomatsu), a stone-carver from the Song dynasty who had come to Japan, and formally consecrated in the third year of Jogen (1209) in the presence of the Retired Emperor Go-Toba. Technique: a mandorla-shaped recess was carved into the rock face, within which the standing Maitreya figure is rendered in incised relief. The temple grounds are also celebrated as a site of weeping cherry trees (the 'Ko-ito Shidare-zakura,' approximately 300 years old) and house a standing wooden image of Jizo Bosatsu (Important Cultural Property) — associated with a legend in which the deity served as a substitute to save an innocent girl.
highlights_en: A National Historic Site: an 11.5-metre standing Maitreya rendered in incised relief within a mandorla-shaped recess cut into the river-facing cliff face
Commissioned in 1207 and formally consecrated in 1209 in the presence of Retired Emperor Go-Toba
Executed by Yi Xinmo (Igyomatsu) and his workshop — a Song-dynasty stone-carver who had come to Japan
Famous for weeping cherry trees (Ko-ito Shidare-zakura, approximately 300 years old)
A standing wooden Jizo Bosatsu (Important Cultural Property), associated with the legend of the 'substitute Jizo' who saved an innocent girl

name: 鹿高神社（白鹿伝承）
name_kana: かたかじんじゃ（はくろくでんしょう）
address: 三重県名張市安部田1942
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約50分（名阪国道→国道165号経由）
summary: 壬申の乱（672年）に大海人皇子（後の天武天皇）が宇陀川増水で渡れず困窮した際・白鹿が現れて川を渡らせたという伝承の地。主祭神「鹿高神」は三代実録（879年）に記載される国史現在の神。境内古墳は市指定史跡。
highlights: 白鹿伝承：壬申の乱で大海人皇子が宇陀川を白鹿の背に乗って渡ったとされる民間伝承
主祭神「鹿高神（かたかのかみ）」は三代実録・貞観15年（873年）に従五位下を賜った国史現在神
境内に全長42mの前方後円墳（鹿高神社境内古墳・6世紀中頃・市指定史跡）
本居宣長の菅笠日記（1772年）にも「かたか」として言及される歴史的著名地
国道165号線が参道を横断する珍しい立地

deepdive.history_jp: 社伝によれば慶雲3年（706年）3月に「鹿高ノ神を斎る」とある（安部田村由来記）。三代実録・貞観15年（873年）9月条に「正六位から従五位下に進められた」と記録される国史現在の神。もとは「春日神社」という社名であったが・明治40〜41年の合祀政策で周辺の無格社20余社を合祀し「鹿高神社」と改称・現在に至る。
deepdive.history_en: According to local records (Abe-da Village History), the deity Kataka-no-Kami was enshrined in 706 CE. The deity appears in the official chronicle Sandai Jitsuroku (879 CE), promoted from Shoshiinoge to Jūgoinoge rank, confirming its status as a 'kokushi-genzai' (state-recorded) shrine. Originally known as Kasuga Shrine, it was renamed Kataka Jinja after absorbing over 20 smaller shrines during the Meiji-era shrine consolidation policy (1907–1908).
deepdive.religion_jp: 主祭神：鹿高神（かたかのかみ）・天児屋根命・蛭子命・五男三女神ほか合祀神多数。鹿高神は知謀学徳の神として信仰を集める。白鹿伝承との関係で・神使としての白鹿（春日大社と同様の鹿信仰）の系譜に位置づけられる可能性があるが・直接的な史料はなく伝説的性格が強い。
deepdive.religion_en: Primary enshrined deities: Kataka-no-Kami (deity of wisdom and learning) plus Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Ebisu-no-Mikoto, and numerous merged deities from consolidated local shrines. The white deer association parallels Kasuga Taisha's sacred deer tradition, though a direct doctrinal connection is folkloric rather than documented.
deepdive.architecture_jp: 本殿・拝殿からなる標準的な神社建築。境内に鹿高神社境内古墳（全長42mの前方後円墳・後円部径21m・前方部幅26m・6世紀中頃築造・横穴式石室2基・名張市指定史跡1970年指定）が所在。国道165号線が旧参道を横断しており・南側と北側にそれぞれ鳥居が立つ。
deepdive.architecture_en: The shrine consists of a standard honden (main hall) and haiden (oratory) arrangement. Within the precinct stands the Kataka Shrine keyhole-shaped kofun burial mound (total length 42m, dated to mid-6th century, two corridor-style stone chambers, designated Nabari City Historic Site in 1970). National Route 165 bisects the former approach road, with torii gates standing on both the south and north sides.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 鹿高神社境内古墳：名張市指定史跡（1970年・昭和45年指定）。全長42mの前方後円墳。社殿自体の文化財指定は確認されていない。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: The Kataka Shrine kofun (burial mound, total length 42m, mid-6th century) is designated as a Nabari City Historic Site (1970). No designation status has been confirmed for the shrine buildings themselves.
deepdive.legends_jp: 壬申の乱（672年）に大海人皇子が吉野から伊賀を目指して進軍中・この地で宇陀川が増水して渡れなかった。そこに白い大鹿が現れ・皇子はその背に乗って川を渡った。川の水は奇跡的に減り・供の者たちも続いて渡ることができた。白鹿はその後・矢川の春日神社の山へ姿を消したとも伝わる。この地は「鹿の里」と呼ばれていたが「鹿高（かたか）」と呼ぶようになったという。本居宣長の菅笠日記（安永元年/1772年）にもこの地への言及がある。
deepdive.legends_en: During the Jinshin War (672 CE), Prince Oama (the future Emperor Tenmu) was marching from Yoshino toward Iga when he found the Uda River flooded and impassable. A great white deer appeared and carried the prince safely across; miraculously the water receded, allowing his retinue to follow. The deer is said to have disappeared into the mountains of Yanagawa Kasuga Shrine. This place, called 'Deer Village' (Shika-no-sato), came to be known as Kataka ('Deer Heights'). The scholar-poet Motoori Norinaga recorded a visit here in his 1772 travelogue Sugegasa Nikki.
deepdive.access_jp: 近鉄大阪線・名張駅から奈良交通バス（榛原行き）で約15分・国道165号線の「鹿高橋」付近下車。またはJR・近鉄・各ICから国道165号線で名張市安部田方面。亀山ICから車で約50分（名阪国道を西へ→国道165号線）。境内に駐車スペースあり。
deepdive.access_en: From Kintetsu Nabari Station, take a Nara Kotsu bus toward Haibara and alight near the 'Kataka Bridge' on Route 165 (approximately 15 minutes). By car from Kameyama IC, head west on the Meihan Expressway and join Route 165 (approximately 50 minutes). Limited parking is available within the shrine grounds.
deepdive.photo_points_jp: ①境内古墳（前方後円墳）全景：樹木が繁茂しているため墳丘の形状が分かりにくい場合がある。②国道165号線を挟む2つの鳥居：珍しい立地を記録する意味で撮影価値あり。③本殿周辺の杉木立。
deepdive.photo_points_en: 1. The kofun burial mound (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the precincts: note that dense vegetation may obscure the mound profile. 2. The two torii gates straddling Route 165: a documenting shot capturing the unusual layout. 3. The cedar grove around the main hall.
deepdive.nearby_jp: 名張市街（名張駅周辺）・赤目四十八滝（車で約20分）・大和路沿いの石仏群・室生寺（車で約30分）。
deepdive.nearby_en: Nabari city center (near Nabari Station); Akame 48 Waterfalls (approximately 20 minutes by car); Muro-ji Temple (approximately 30 minutes by car).
deepdive.warnings_jp: 国道165号線が境内を横断しているため参道横断時は自動車に注意。境内古墳への立入は植生保護のため節度ある行動を。
deepdive.warnings_en: National Route 165 bisects the shrine precinct — exercise caution when crossing the road on the traditional approach path. Respect the vegetation and soil of the burial mound when viewing.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 白鹿伝承は地元の昔話として伝わる民俗伝承であり・歴史的事実を直接証明する史料は存在しない。「不確かな情報：」祭神「鹿高神」と白鹿伝承の神話的関連性については神社側からの公式説明が確認できておらず・伝承上の結びつきと見るべきである。三代実録への記載は確実な史料。

summary_en: The site associated with a tradition in which, during the Jinshin War of 672 CE, Prince Oama (later Emperor Tenmu) found himself unable to cross the flooded Uda River, whereupon a white deer appeared and carried him across. The principal deity, 'Shikataka-no-Kami' (Deer-High Deity), is recorded as a historical deity in the Sandai Jitsuroku (Three Reigns' True Records, 879 CE), having been granted the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in the fifteenth year of Jogan (873). The precinct contains a 42-metre keyhole-shaped burial mound (Shikataka Jinja precincts tumulus, mid-sixth century, designated a municipal historic site). The site is also mentioned as 'Kataka' in Motoori Norinaga's Sugagasa Diary (1772). National Route 165 crosses through the approach path — an unusual spatial arrangement.
highlights_en: A folk tradition holding that Prince Oama crossed the flooded Uda River on the back of a white deer during the Jinshin War of 672 CE
The principal deity Shikataka-no-Kami was granted the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in the Sandai Jitsuroku (Jogan 15, 873 CE) — a 'historically attested deity'
A 42-metre keyhole-shaped burial mound within the precincts (mid-sixth century, designated a municipal historic site)
Mentioned as 'Kataka' in Motoori Norinaga's Sugagasa Diary (1772) — a historically notable site
National Route 165 bisects the shrine approach — an unusual spatial arrangement

name: 海住山寺・恭仁京跡
name_kana: かいじゅうせんじ・くにきょうあと
name_en: Kaijusen-ji Temple and the Kuni-kyo Palace Site
address: 京都府木津川市加茂町例幣海住山境外20（海住山寺）／京都府木津川市加茂町例幣（恭仁宮跡）
status: 現存（海住山寺拝観可・恭仁宮跡常時公開）
fee: 海住山寺：本堂内拝観500円（入山料含む）・散策のみ入山料200円　恭仁宮跡：無料
hours: 海住山寺：9:00〜16:30　恭仁宮跡：常時開放
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約65分（名阪国道→国道163号→県道経由）
summary: 奈良時代の離宮・恭仁宮跡（特別史跡）と・その東山腹に位置する鎌倉時代の国宝五重塔を有する古刹・海住山寺をセットで巡る。恭仁宮は740〜744年に聖武天皇が一時遷都した幻の都城。海住山寺は貞慶（解脱上人）が中興した修行の場。
highlights: 海住山寺国宝五重塔（鎌倉時代・建保2年/1214年・高さ17.7m・裳階付き・室生寺五重塔に次ぐ日本二番目の小ささ）
恭仁宮跡（特別史跡・740〜744年の聖武天皇による幻の遷都地・現在は大極殿跡の礎石群が残る）
貞慶（解脱上人）による海住山寺の中興（承元2年/1208年）・笠置寺から移り晩年5年を過ごした
海住山寺重要文化財群：木造十一面観音立像（本尊・平安時代）・木造四天王立像（鎌倉時代）ほか多数
恭仁宮大極殿跡の礎石群と山城国分寺の塔跡礎石が隣接

deepdive.history_jp: 【海住山寺】寺伝では天平7年（735年）聖武天皇の勅願で良弁を開山として「藤尾山観音寺」として開創。史実として確認できるのは鎌倉時代以降で・承元2年（1208年）に貞慶（解脱上人）が笠置寺から移り「補陀洛山海住山寺」と改称・中興した。貞慶の没後（建暦3年/1213年）・弟子の覚真が継ぎ・建保2年（1214年）に五重塔が完成。室町時代には塔頭58か坊を数えた。【恭仁宮跡】天平12年（740年）に聖武天皇が藤原広嗣の乱の後・平城京から遷都した幻の都城。4年後の天平16年（744年）に難波宮へさらに遷都。恭仁宮は廃止されて山城国分寺の伽藍が整備され・その後は荒廃した。
deepdive.history_en: Kaijusenji: Temple tradition records founding in 735 CE as Fujioo-zan Kannon-ji by Ryoben at Emperor Shomu's behest, though this is undocumented. The verifiable history begins with Jokei (Gedatsu Shonin), who relocated from Kasagi-dera in 1208 and renamed the temple Fudarakusan Kaijusenji. After Jokei's death in 1213, his disciple Kakushin oversaw completion of the five-storied pagoda in 1214. By the Muromachi period, 58 sub-temples existed. Kuni Palace: In 740 CE, Emperor Shomu relocated the capital from Heijokyo to Kuni following the Fujiwara Hirotsugu Rebellion; this 'Kuni-kyo' was abandoned in 744. Its site became the Yamashiro Kokubunji (provincial temple), ruins of which survive.
deepdive.religion_jp: 海住山寺：真言宗智山派（近世まで興福寺末）。本尊は十一面観音（重要文化財・平安時代・10世紀頃の造像）。貞慶は法相宗系（興福寺出身）だが当寺は後に真言宗に転じた。恭仁宮跡は宗教施設ではなく奈良時代の宮都遺跡だが・朝廷の仏教保護政策のもとで山城国分寺が同地に建立された歴史的関連がある。
deepdive.religion_en: Kaijusenji belongs to the Chisan branch of Shingon Buddhism (formerly affiliated with Kofuku-ji, Hosso school, until the Meiji era). The principal icon is an 11-faced Kannon (Juichimen Kannon) of the 10th century, designated an Important Cultural Property. The Kuni Palace site itself is a secular Nara-period imperial ruin, though the subsequent Yamashiro Kokubunji (provincial temple) established there reflects Emperor Shomu's nationwide Buddhist patronage policy.
deepdive.architecture_jp: 【国宝五重塔】鎌倉時代・建保2年（1214年）完成。高さ17.7m（基壇上面〜相輪上端）。初層に裳階（もこし）を付す現存する平安〜鎌倉時代の五重塔としては唯一の例（法隆寺塔は飛鳥時代）。各重とも二手先組物。心柱は初層天井より上から立つ特殊構造。【重要文化財】文殊堂（鎌倉時代）・木造十一面観音立像（本堂・平安時代・像高189cm）ほか。【恭仁宮跡】大極殿跡の礎石が現存・隣接して山城国分寺の塔跡礎石群あり。
deepdive.architecture_en: Five-storied pagoda (National Treasure, 1214 CE): 17.7m tall, the second-smallest surviving wooden pagoda in Japan after Muro-ji's. Its distinctive feature is the mokoshi (skirt roof) added beneath the first-floor eave — unique among extant Heian–Kamakura period five-story pagodas; the only comparable example (Horyuji) dates to the Asuka period. The pagoda has no heart pillar in the ground story, instead rising from above the first-floor ceiling. Kuni Palace ruins: foundation stones of the Daigokuden (Great Audience Hall) survive, flanked by the equally ancient foundations of the Yamashiro Kokubunji pagoda.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 海住山寺：国宝（五重塔）・重要文化財（文殊堂・木造十一面観音立像・木造四天王立像・海住山寺文書24通ほか）・京都府指定文化財複数。恭仁宮跡：特別史跡（文化庁指定）。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: Kaijusenji: One National Treasure (five-storied pagoda); multiple Important Cultural Properties including Monju-do hall, the Juichimen Kannon principal icon, Four Heavenly Kings statues, and 24 temple documents. Kuni Palace site: designated a Special National Historic Site by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
deepdive.legends_jp: 【海住山寺】寺号「海住山寺」の由来：観音の誓願が海のように広大であることを「海」に喩え・その誓願に安住する山という意味を込めた（明本抄「良算聞書」）。また補陀洛山（南方海中の観音浄土）になぞらえた呼称でもある。【恭仁宮】天平15年（743年）に大仏建立の詔を発した聖武天皇が一時この地に都を置いたが・大仏の完成を見ることなく難波宮へ去り・再び平城京に戻った。幻の都城として歴史上に残る。
deepdive.legends_en: Kaijusenji name: According to the 'Ryozan Kikigaki' record in the Myohonsho, the 'sea' (umi) in the temple name represents Kannon's boundless vow to save sentient beings, while the 'mountain' (yama) signifies dwelling in peaceful contemplation of that vow — also evoking Potaraka, the legendary island paradise of Kannon in the southern sea. Kuni Palace: Emperor Shomu briefly moved his capital here in 740 CE amid political upheaval, then moved again to Naniwa in 744, abandoning Kuni as a capital after only four years — making it one of history's most ephemeral imperial cities.
deepdive.access_jp: 【海住山寺】JR加茂駅から奈良交通バス「和束町小杉」行きで3分・岡崎下車・徒歩40分（急坂あり）。または木津川市コミュニティバス「海住山寺口」下車徒歩25分（平日のみ・本数少ない）。実質的に自家用車またはタクシーが現実的。駐車場あり。【恭仁宮跡】JR加茂駅から徒歩約15分。国道163号線沿い。
deepdive.access_en: Kaijusenji: From JR Kamo Station, take a Nara Kotsu bus 3 minutes to Okazaki, then walk 40 minutes uphill (steep in places). The Kizugawa City Community Bus stops at 'Kaijusenji-guchi' (25 minutes' walk; weekdays only, infrequent service). Practically speaking, a private car or taxi is the most realistic option. Parking is available. Kuni Palace ruins: approximately 15 minutes' walk from JR Kamo Station, along Route 163.
deepdive.photo_points_jp: ①国宝五重塔：参道から見上げる構図が定番。春の新緑・秋の紅葉時期が最良。②大極殿跡の礎石群：広大な草地に礎石が点在し・奈良時代の都城規模を体感できる。③恭仁宮跡から海住山寺方面への山並み。
deepdive.photo_points_en: 1. The National Treasure pagoda: classic upward composition from the approach path; best in spring green or autumn foliage. 2. The Daigokuden foundation stones amid open grassland — convey the scale of the Nara-period palace complex. 3. Views toward the hillside where Kaijusenji sits, seen from the palace site.
deepdive.nearby_jp: 笠置寺（車で約20分）・蟹満寺（車で約15分）・神童寺（車で約20分）・当尾地区磨崖仏群（車で約15分）・JR加茂駅周辺。
deepdive.nearby_en: Kasagi-dera Temple (approximately 20 minutes by car); Kanimanzaiji Temple (approximately 15 minutes by car); Shintoji Temple (approximately 20 minutes by car); Tono-o rock Buddha reliefs (approximately 15 minutes by car).
deepdive.warnings_jp: 海住山寺は山腹にあり・公共交通でのアクセスは本数が少なく急勾配の徒歩が必要。高齢者・体力に不安がある方は車またはタクシーを推奨。恭仁宮跡は草地が広いため夏季は虫・熱中症対策が必要。
deepdive.warnings_en: Kaijusenji is on a hillside with infrequent public transport and a long steep walk — car or taxi recommended for those with mobility limitations or in hot weather. The Kuni Palace ruins are extensive open grassland; bring insect repellent and sun protection in summer.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 海住山寺の境内での三脚使用は本堂拝観申込者のみ可能（散策のみの入山者は不可）。ハイキング等での境内での食事・休憩・写真撮影も本堂拝観申込者に限られる旨が公式サイトに明記されている。

summary_en: A paired itinerary combining the Kuni-no-miya palace site (Special Historic Site) — a temporary capital established by Emperor Shomu between 740 and 744 CE — with the nearby Kaijusen-ji Temple, a medieval foundation possessing a National Treasure five-storey pagoda on the eastern hillside. The Kuni-no-miya was one of several temporary capitals during Emperor Shomu's reign; today only the foundation stones of the great audience hall (daigokuden) site survive. At Kaijusen-ji, the monk Jokei (Gedatsu Shonin) re-established the temple in the second year of Jogen (1208), and the five-storey pagoda — built in the second year of Kenpo (1214) and standing 17.7 metres with a skirt-eave (mokoshi) — is the second-smallest in Japan after Muro-ji's. The temple treasury holds numerous Important Cultural Properties, including the principal image, an eleven-faced Kannon (Heian period), and a set of Four Heavenly Kings (Kamakura period).
highlights_en: The Kaijusen-ji National Treasure five-storey pagoda (Kamakura period, Kenpo 2/1214, 17.7 m, with skirt-eave) — the second-smallest five-storey pagoda in Japan, after Muro-ji's
The Kuni-no-miya palace site (Special Historic Site): the ephemeral capital of Emperor Shomu (740–744 CE), now represented by the remaining foundation stones of the daigokuden hall
Jokei (Gedatsu Shonin) re-established Kaijusen-ji in 1208 after relocating from Kasagi-dera, spending his final five years here
Important Cultural Properties at Kaijusen-ji: the principal wooden eleven-faced Kannon (Heian period), a set of wooden Four Heavenly Kings (Kamakura period), and numerous others
The Kuni-no-miya daigokuden foundation stones and the adjacent pagoda ruins foundation stones of Yamashiro Kokubunji stand in close proximity

name: 蟹満寺
name_kana: かにまんじ
address: 京都府木津川市山城町綺田浜36
fee: 無料（国宝像の外観拝観）／本尊特別拝観時は別途料金の場合あり
hours: 日中拝観可（詳細は要問合せ）
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約60分（名阪国道→国道163号経由）
summary: 蟹の恩返し説話（今昔物語集巻16）の伝承地。本尊・国宝銅造釈迦如来坐像（高さ240cm・重さ2.2トン）は2005年調査で白鳳期（7世紀末）の旧仏であることが確認された。7世紀末の発掘調査では大規模な古代寺院の痕跡も判明。
highlights: 国宝銅造釈迦如来坐像（高さ240cm・重さ2.2トン・白鳳期7世紀末・この年代の大型金銅仏は日本に4体のみ）
2005年調査で本尊が白鳳創建以来の旧仏（当初本尊）であると確認
蟹の恩返し縁起：今昔物語集に収録された仏教説話で蟹が恩人の娘を蛇から救う内容
毎年4月18日の蟹供養放生会（沢蟹を放生する行事）
1990年発掘調査で7世紀末の大規模古代寺院の遺構を確認

deepdive.history_jp: 歴史的起源は明確でない。1990年の発掘調査で7世紀末頃（白鳳期）に現在より大規模な寺院が存在した痕跡が確認され・国家の勅令または地域の大氏族による建立が想定されている。創建時の寺名は「紙幢多寺」または「綺寺（かばたてら）」と古文書に見られる。その後荒廃し・平安時代後期に「蟹の恩返し」の仏教説話が当地と結びついて「蟹満寺」という寺名に変わったとされる。本尊釈迦如来坐像については2005年の調査で白鳳期当初の本尊であった説が有力となった。
deepdive.history_en: The temple's origins are unclear. A 1990 excavation revealed evidence of a substantially larger temple complex from the late 7th century (Hakuho period), suggesting construction by imperial command or a major regional clan. The original temple name recorded in old documents is 'Kahadatera' or 'Shikidō-ta-ji'. The temple subsequently fell into decline; by the late Heian period the 'grateful crab' Buddhist tale had become associated with the site, and the temple was renamed 'Kanimanzaiji' (Temple Full of Crabs). A 2005 investigation confirmed that the bronze Shakyamuni statue was the original main icon from the Hakuho-period founding.
deepdive.religion_jp: 宗派は天台宗系・現在は単立。本尊は釈迦如来（坐像・国宝）。蟹の恩返し説話は観音信仰と結びつく仏教説話（今昔物語集巻16）で・慈愛深い信者が蟹を救い・その蟹が蛇（危難）から娘を救うという報恩の構造を持つ。蟹供養放生会（4月18日）は生命の尊重と報恩を実践する行事として現在も継続されている。
deepdive.religion_en: The temple is currently independent (single-temple denomination). The principal icon is a seated Shakyamuni Buddha (National Treasure). The 'grateful crab' narrative (from the Konjaku Monogatarishu, compiled around 1120) is a Buddhist tale of compassion and karmic reward: a devout girl rescues crabs, who later transform (through Kannon's divine aid) to slay a snake threatening her life. The annual Crab Toro-nagashi ceremony (April 18) — releasing live freshwater crabs — perpetuates this tradition.
deepdive.architecture_jp: 現在は控えめな規模の境内に本堂一棟。1990年発掘調査により7世紀末の大規模伽藍跡（現在より大きな寺院）が確認されている。国宝銅造釈迦如来坐像：高さ240cm・重さ2.2トン・铸造技法は飛鳥〜白鳳時代の特徴を有する。奈良薬師寺金堂の薬師像と比肩されうる白鳳彫刻の秀作（木津川市公式）。
deepdive.architecture_en: The current temple is modest in scale with a single main hall. The 1990 excavation confirmed a 7th-century precinct substantially larger than the present complex. The principal icon — a bronze seated Shakyamuni, 240cm tall, weighing 2.2 tonnes — exhibits casting techniques characteristic of the Asuka–Hakuho transition. Kizugawa City officially describes it as comparable in quality to the Yakushiji Yakushi triad in Nara.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 国宝：銅造釈迦如来坐像（白鳳期・高さ240cm・重さ2.2t）。2005年の近年調査では「蟹満寺本尊は白鳳創建以来の旧仏であった」ことが判明（木津川市公式）。なお像の白鳳期制作の根拠は・鋳造技法・表現様式・発掘された7世紀末の遺構との対応に基づくものであり・銘文等による絶対年代は確認されていない（様式論的推定）。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: National Treasure: bronze seated Shakyamuni Buddha (Hakuho period, 240cm, 2.2t). The 2005 survey concluded the statue was the original main icon from the Hakuho-period founding. Note: the Hakuho-period attribution is based on casting technique, stylistic analysis, and correspondence with 7th-century excavation evidence — there is no dated inscription confirming the absolute year of manufacture (the dating is art-historical/typological).
deepdive.legends_jp: 「蟹の恩返し」説話（今昔物語集巻16収録）：観音を深く信仰するある娘が・村人が蟹をいじめているのを助けた。のちに父親が蛇にカエルを飲み込まれる場面に遭遇し・うっかり娘を嫁にやると約束して助けてしまう。夜に蛇がその約束を迫りに来るが・夜明けに外へ出ると大きな蛇と多数の蟹が死んでいた。家族は観音が娘の助けた蟹に姿を変えて娘を守ったのだと悟り・蟹と蛇を埋葬して御堂を建てたという。別ヴァージョン（南山城の古寺巡礼サイト）では観音が蟹に変身したとされている。
deepdive.legends_en: The 'Grateful Crab' tale (Konjaku Monogatarishu Book 16): A girl deeply devoted to Kannon rescued crabs from a villager's grasp. Later, her father rashly promised his daughter in marriage to a snake in exchange for saving a frog. That night the snake came to claim the promise; but at dawn the family found the snake dead, surrounded by many dead crabs. They believed Kannon had transformed into the crabs to protect the girl, and erected a hall in gratitude. A variant recorded by Minamiyamashiro-koji.jp has Kannon appearing directly in the form of crabs.
deepdive.access_jp: JR奈良線・棚倉駅より徒歩約20分（平坦な農道）。JR学研都市線（片町線）・奈良線・関西線・木津駅からタクシー約15分。JR学研都市線・祝園駅または近鉄・新祝園駅からタクシー約10分。
deepdive.access_en: From JR Tanakura Station (Nara Line), approximately 20 minutes' walk (flat agricultural road). By taxi from JR Kizu Station (approximately 15 minutes) or JR/Kintetsu Shukugawa/Shin-Shukugawa Station (approximately 10 minutes).
deepdive.photo_points_jp: ①本堂正面と境内の緑。②蟹をモチーフにした境内の石彫・案内板。③4月18日の蟹供養放生会（沢蟹放生の瞬間）：事前確認要。
deepdive.photo_points_en: 1. The main hall facade with its surrounding greenery. 2. Stone carvings and signs featuring crab motifs within the precinct. 3. The April 18 Crab Release Ceremony (kani kuyo hojo-e): confirm details in advance.
deepdive.nearby_jp: 神童寺（車で約10分）・海住山寺（車で約15分）・恭仁宮跡（車で約15分）・棚倉駅周辺（徒歩圏）。
deepdive.nearby_en: Shintoji Temple (approximately 10 minutes by car); Kaijusenji Temple (approximately 15 minutes by car); Kuni Palace ruins (approximately 15 minutes by car); JR Tanakura Station (within walking distance).
deepdive.warnings_jp: 本尊国宝像の特別公開・拝観可否は事前に寺院へ問合せること（常時公開されているわけではない場合がある）。
deepdive.warnings_en: Availability of close viewing of the National Treasure statue should be confirmed with the temple in advance, as special exhibitions may be required for interior access.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 不確かな情報：本尊の白鳳期制作の根拠については「2005年の近年調査で白鳳創建以来の旧仏と確認」（木津川市公式）とあるが・鋳造様式に基づく推定であり銘文等による絶対年代証明はない（様式論的推定）。像がもともとこの寺にあったのか他から移されたのかについても「元々の本尊」説が有力だが学術的に確定していない旨を注記。

summary_en: The site associated with the crab-repayment-of-kindness narrative preserved in the Konjaku Monogatarishu (Vol. 16). The principal image, a National Treasure bronze seated Shakyamuni (height 240 cm, weight 2.2 tonnes), was confirmed by a 2005 investigation to be an original image from the Hakuho period (late seventh century) and has remained in the temple since its founding — a rare instance of a large-scale gilt-bronze image surviving in situ from the period of a temple's construction. As of that survey, only four large gilt-bronze images of this date are known to survive in Japan. The founding temple itself, confirmed by a 1990 excavation to have been a substantial ancient temple complex of the late seventh century, provides the institutional context for this exceptional object. Each year on 18 April, a 'crab merit-release ceremony' (kani kuyo hojo-e) is held, during which river crabs are released.
highlights_en: A National Treasure bronze seated Shakyamuni (height 240 cm, weight 2.2 tonnes, Hakuho period, late seventh century): only four large-scale gilt-bronze images of this date survive in Japan
The 2005 investigation confirmed the principal image to be the original image installed at the time of the temple's founding — it has remained here since the late seventh century
The crab-repayment-of-kindness origin legend: a Buddhist narrative in the Konjaku Monogatarishu in which crabs rescue the daughter of their benefactor from a serpent
An annual crab merit-release ceremony (hojo-e) is held on 18 April
A 1990 excavation confirmed the remains of a large-scale ancient temple complex dating to the late seventh century

name: 神童寺
name_kana: しんどうじ
address: 京都府木津川市山城町神童子不晴谷112
fee: 境内自由（収蔵庫拝観は事前確認要）
hours: 日中拝観（詳細は要確認）
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約60分（名阪国道→国道163号経由）
summary: 吉野と密接な関係を持つ修験道の古刹。本堂（重要文化財・1406年再建）を中心に重要文化財の仏像群を収蔵。本尊の蔵王権現像は役行者が神童の助けを借りて刻んだと伝わる。開基については役行者説と聖徳太子説が併存し両者の史料的根拠は弱い。
highlights: 本堂（蔵王堂）：室町時代・応永13年（1406年）再建・国重要文化財
木造不動明王立像（重要文化財・平安時代・「波切白不動尊」・白色の不動明王は日本唯一）
木造愛染明王坐像（重要文化財・平安時代・「天弓愛染」・天に向かって矢を射る姿は日本に4体のみ）
修験道の霊地・大和国吉野の金峰山と深い縁を持つ北吉野山
有栖川宮御祈願所・菊花御紋章の幕を下賜された格式ある寺院

deepdive.history_jp: 「北吉野山神童寺縁起」によれば聖徳太子の創建・大観世音教寺と称したという。別の伝承では天武天皇4年（675年）に役行者が入山・2人の神童の助力で蔵王権現像を刻み「神童教護国寺」と称したとされる。この地に吉野金峰山を拠点とする修験者集団の勢力が及び修験道の道場として栄えた。治承4年（1180年）平資盛の兵火で一山26坊が全焼。建久元年（1190年）源頼朝が再建。元弘元年（1331年）元弘の乱で再び焼失・応永13年（1406年）興福寺官務懐乗越前が現在の本堂（蔵王堂）を再建した。江戸時代には有栖川宮御祈願所として菊花御紋章の幕を下賜。
deepdive.history_en: According to the 'Kitagino-zan Shintoji Engi' record, the temple was founded by Prince Shotoku as 'Daikanzeon Kyoji.' A separate tradition attributes its establishment to En-no-Gyoja (675 CE), who carved the Zaō Gongen image with the assistance of two divine children (shindō), giving the temple its name. The site became a Shugendo training ground connected to Mt. Kinpusen in Yoshino. In 1180, forces of Taira-no-Sukemori burned all 26 sub-temples; Minamoto-no-Yoritomo funded reconstruction in 1190. The complex burned again in the 1331 Genko War, and the current main hall was rebuilt in 1406. During the Edo period the temple served as a prayer hall for the Arisugawa-no-Miya imperial family.
deepdive.religion_jp: 真言宗智山派。本尊：蔵王権現（修験道の本尊・役行者が刻んだとされる）。大和国吉野・金峰山の修験道と深い関係を持ち「北吉野山」の山号もそれを示す。蔵王権現は釈迦如来（過去）・千手観音（現在）・弥勒菩薩（未来）の三仏を一体に顕した修験道固有の本尊。
deepdive.religion_en: The temple belongs to the Chisan branch of Shingon Buddhism. The main icon is Zaō Gongen, the principal deity of Shugendo — a syncretic figure combining Shakyamuni (past), Senju Kannon (present), and Maitreya (future). The temple's mountain name 'Kitagino-zan' (Northern Yoshino Mountain) reflects its deep ties to the Shugendo tradition of Mt. Kinpusen (Yoshino).
deepdive.architecture_jp: 本堂（蔵王堂）：応永13年（1406年）興福寺官務懐乗越前による再建・国重要文化財（附：棟札・銘札・旧鬼瓦）。護摩堂（木津川市指定有形文化財・江戸時代）。表門（木津川市指定有形文化財・江戸時代）。境内に十三重石塔・五重石塔・地蔵堂・鐘楼・収蔵庫。
deepdive.architecture_en: Main Hall (Zaō-dō): rebuilt in 1406 by Kofuku-ji administrator Kainojo, designated a National Important Cultural Property (with attached ridge plaques and architectural remnants). Goma-dō (fire ritual hall) and Omon (main gate) are designated Kizugawa City Tangible Cultural Properties (Edo period). The precinct also contains 13-storied and 5-storied stone pagodas, a Jizo hall, a bell tower, and a dedicated storage hall for the statues.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 重要文化財（国指定）：本堂（蔵王堂・1406年）・木造愛染明王坐像（平安時代・天弓愛染・天に向かって矢を射る姿は日本に4体のみ・白色の重文は本像のみ）・木造不動明王立像（平安時代・波切白不動尊・白色の不動明王は日本に本像のみ）・木造阿弥陀如来坐像（平安時代）・木造毘沙門天立像（平安時代）・木造日光菩薩立像（平安時代）・木造月光菩薩立像（平安時代）・木造伎楽面（1196年）。木津川市指定有形文化財：護摩堂・表門。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: National Important Cultural Properties: the main hall (1406); the Seated Aizen Myo-o (Tenyu Aizen, Heian period — one of only four surviving images of this form in Japan, the only white one); the Standing Fudo Myo-o (Namikirihaku Fudo, Heian period — the only surviving white Fudo Myo-o among the four known in Japan); plus Amida Nyorai, Bishamonten, Nikko and Gakko Bosatsu statues and a Gigaku mask (1196). The Goma-do and main gate are designated city-level cultural properties.
deepdive.legends_jp: ①役行者伝承（675年）：役行者が山中で修行中に2人の神童が現れ・その助力で蔵王権現像を刻んだ。「神童」の助けを得たことが寺名「神童寺」の由来。②聖徳太子開基伝承：「北吉野山神童寺縁起」に基づく。両者の史料的根拠はいずれも弱く・縁起文書の記述に依拠するもの（後世の作成の可能性が高い）。
deepdive.legends_en: 1. En-no-Gyoja legend (675 CE): During mountain practice, two divine children (shindō) appeared and helped En-no-Gyoja carve the Zaō Gongen icon, giving the temple its name 'Shintoji' (Divine Children Temple). 2. Prince Shotoku founding legend: derived from the temple's Engi (founding record). Both legends lack corroborating contemporary documents.
deepdive.access_jp: JR奈良線・棚倉駅より徒歩約40分（山道あり）または車で約10分。JR奈良線・棚倉駅からタクシーも利用可。木津川市中心部から車で約15分。公共交通でのアクセスは本数が少なく困難なため・車またはタクシーが推奨される。
deepdive.access_en: From JR Tanakura Station, approximately 40 minutes on foot (including mountain path) or 10 minutes by car/taxi. Public transport options are limited and infrequent — private car or taxi from JR Tanakura Station is strongly recommended.
deepdive.photo_points_jp: ①本堂（蔵王堂）正面：室町時代の建築様式が残る。②収蔵庫内の重要文化財仏像群（拝観可能時）：白色の不動明王・天弓愛染明王は特に希少。撮影可否は事前確認。③境内の石塔群・山門。
deepdive.photo_points_en: 1. The main hall (Zaō-dō) facade: Muromachi-period architectural style. 2. The Important Cultural Property statues in the treasure hall (when accessible for viewing): the white Fudo and the Aizen with bow are exceptionally rare nationally. Confirm photography permission. 3. The stone pagodas and gate within the precinct.
deepdive.nearby_jp: 蟹満寺（車で約10分）・海住山寺（車で約15分）・棚倉駅周辺。
deepdive.nearby_en: Kanimanzaiji Temple (approximately 10 minutes by car); Kaijusenji Temple (approximately 15 minutes by car); JR Tanakura Station area.
deepdive.warnings_jp: 公共交通でのアクセスが困難。JR棚倉駅から徒歩40分は山道を含むため・雨天・冬季は足元に注意。収蔵庫の開扉・拝観スケジュールは不定期のため事前に問合せが必要。
deepdive.warnings_en: Public transport access is extremely limited. The 40-minute walk from JR Tanakura Station includes mountain terrain — exercise caution in wet or wintry conditions. The statue storage hall is not always open; confirm opening schedule before visiting.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 役行者開基（675年）・聖徳太子開基の両伝承は「不確かな情報」であり・いずれも後世に作成された縁起文書に依拠するもので同時代史料による裏付けはない。両伝承が縁起上で「並立」している点も史料的な複雑さを示している。訪問記事等でこれらを確定事実として記述することは避けること。

summary_en: An ancient temple of the mountain-ascetic (Shugendo) tradition with close ties to Yoshino. The main hall (Important Cultural Property, rebuilt in 1406) houses a collection of Important Cultural Property Buddhist images. The principal image, a Zaodo Gongen, is said to have been carved by En-no-Gyoja with the assistance of a divine child (shindo), from which the temple's name derives. Most notable among the sculptural holdings is a standing wooden Fudo Myo-o (Important Cultural Property, Heian period) — known as the 'Wave-cutting White Fudo' — unique in Japan as a white-coloured Fudo image. Also held is a seated wooden Aizen Myo-o (Important Cultural Property, Heian period) in the 'Heavenly-bow' form, one of only four images in Japan depicted with a bow aimed skyward. The temple, known as 'Kita-Yoshino-yama' (Northern Yoshino Mountain), maintains deep sacred ties to Kinpusen in Yamato Province and served as a place of imperial petition by the Arisugawa-no-miya house.
highlights_en: Main hall (Zaodo-do): rebuilt in the thirteenth year of Oei (1406) — a National Important Cultural Property
Standing wooden Fudo Myo-o (Important Cultural Property, Heian period) — known as the 'Wave-cutting White Fudo': unique in Japan as a white-coloured Fudo image
Seated wooden Aizen Myo-o (Important Cultural Property, Heian period) in the 'heavenly-bow' form — one of only four such images in Japan depicted with a bow aimed skyward
A sacred site of Shugendo mountain practice, with deep ties to Kinpusen in Yamato-no-kuni (Yoshino): known as 'Northern Yoshino Mountain'
Designated a place of prayer by the Arisugawa-no-miya imperial house, which donated a curtain bearing the chrysanthemum imperial crest

name: 阿紀神社
name_kana: あきじんじゃ
name_en: Aki Jinja Shrine (Kagiroi Phenomenon and Man'yoshu Poetry)
address: 奈良県宇陀市大宇陀迫間252
hours: 参拝自由（能舞台使用時は除く）
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約70分（名阪国道→国道165号→宇陀市方面経由）
summary: 垂仁天皇の御代に倭姫命が天照大神を祀った「宇多の吾城（阿騎）宮」が起源と伝わる式内社。万葉集巻1の柿本人麻呂の「かぎろひ」の歌（東の野に炎の立つ見えて）の詠まれた「阿騎野」に位置し・冬至前後の早朝に「かぎろひ（陽炎のような光の揺らぎ）」現象を観察できる地。境内には江戸時代の能舞台が現存。
highlights: 万葉集巻1・46番「東の野に炎（かぎろひ）の立つ見えて かへり見すれば月傾きぬ」（柿本人麻呂）の詠まれた阿騎野に所在
かぎろひ現象：冬至前後の早朝に東の空に現れる曙光と陽炎状の光の揺らぎ（毎年12月中旬〜1月にかぎろひ観望会が催される）
式内社・旧県社（1902年昇格）・垂仁天皇の御代に倭姫命が天照大神を祀った地との伝承
祭神：天照皇大神・秋田比売神・邇邇杵命・八意思兼神（主殿）ほか
境内に現存する江戸時代前期建設の能舞台（あきの蛍能・毎年6月中旬に催される）

deepdive.history_jp: 社伝では神代に神楽岡に創建され・崇神天皇の勅によって「神戸大神宮」の号を賜ったとされる。垂仁天皇の御代に皇女・倭姫命が天照大神を各地に祀って歩いた際・宇多の「阿騎（あき）宮」に天照大神を祀ったことが当社の起源とされる。安土桃山時代・天正年間（1573〜1592年）に本郷川西岸の現在地に遷座し「阿紀神社」と改称。1902年（明治35年）に郷社から県社へ昇格。
deepdive.history_en: Temple tradition holds that the shrine was originally founded in the mythological age on Kaguraoka Hill, and received the appellation 'Kando Daijingu' by imperial decree during Emperor Sujin's reign. According to tradition, Princess Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto (niece of Emperor Suinin) enshrined Amaterasu-Omikami here at 'Aki no Miya' in Uda during her journeys. In the Tensho era (1573–1592) the shrine relocated to its present site west of the Hongo River and took its current name Aki Jinja. It was promoted to prefecture-level shrine status in 1902.
deepdive.religion_jp: 祭神（主殿）：天照皇大神（天照大神）・秋田比売神・邇邇杵命・八意思兼神。相殿：天水分神・菅原道真・金山彦神・金山姫神。天照皇大神を主祭神とすることは伊勢神宮外宮の性格と重なり・「倭姫命の巡行地」としての聖性に基づく。秋田比売神は穀物・秋の恵みの神格と見られるが詳細は不確か。
deepdive.religion_en: Main enshrined deities: Amaterasu-Omikami, Akita-hime-no-Kami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, and Yagokoro-Omoi-kane-no-Kami. Secondary hall: Ame-no-Mizuwake-no-Kami, Sugawara-no-Michizane, Kanayama-hiko-no-Kami, Kanayama-hime-no-Kami. The primary enshrinement of Amaterasu reflects the shrine's claimed connection to Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto's sacred tour preceding the foundation of Ise Jingu. Note: the precise theological identity of Akita-hime-no-Kami is uncertain.
deepdive.architecture_jp: 神明造りの本殿・能舞台（江戸時代前期建設）が境内に現存。能舞台は「あきの蛍能」（毎年6月中旬）に現役で使用される非常に珍しい境内舞台。杉木立に囲まれた静寂な境内。
deepdive.architecture_en: The main hall follows the shinmei-zukuri (Ise-style) architectural form. The Edo-period Noh stage within the precinct is remarkably well-preserved and still in active use for the 'Akino Hotaru Noh' (firefly Noh performance, mid-June). The precinct is enveloped by old cedars, creating a hushed atmosphere.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 延喜式神名帳（927年）記載の式内社（大和国宇陀郡・阿紀神社）・旧県社。能舞台は木津川市管内ではなく宇陀市所在のため文化財指定状況は別途確認が必要。社殿・能舞台の具体的な文化財指定情報は公式資料では確認できていない（不確かな情報）。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: Listed in the Engishiki (927 CE) as a government-recognized shrine of Yamato Province (Uda District). Formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. Specific designation status of the shrine buildings and Noh stage could not be confirmed from publicly available official sources (unverified information).
deepdive.legends_jp: ①かぎろひ伝承と万葉集：持統天皇5年（691年）旧暦11月17日（冬至に近い日）・軽皇子（後の文武天皇）が阿騎野（あきの）に宿営した際・随行した柿本人麻呂が東の空に「かぎろひ」（曙光・陽炎状の光の揺らぎ）が立つのを見て詠んだとされる。「東の野に炎の立つ見えて かへり見すれば月傾きぬ」（万葉集巻1・46番）。②倭姫命巡行伝承：日本書紀および倭姫命世記に記される・天照大神の鎮座地を求めて各地を旅した倭姫命が阿騎の地にも立ち寄ったとされる。
deepdive.legends_en: 1. Kagirohi and the Manyoshu: In 691 CE (around the winter solstice, by the lunar calendar), Prince Karu (later Emperor Monmu) camped in Aki-no (Akino Plain). His court poet Kakinomoto-no-Hitomaro composed the famous verse: 'Over the eastern fields I see the crimson glow of flames rising; I look back, and the moon is westering' (Manyoshu Book 1, No. 46). The term 'kagirohi' describes the shimmering mirage-like atmospheric glow observed near sunrise in winter. 2. Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto tradition: The Nihon Shoki and Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto Seiki record that the princess paused at Aki in her search for a permanent home for Amaterasu.
deepdive.access_jp: 近鉄大阪線・大和八木駅から奈良交通バス（宇陀市内バス）で約40分。または名阪国道・針ICより車で約40分。近鉄榛原駅から大宇陀方面へ車で約20分。大宇陀地区の道の駅「宇陀路大宇陀」から徒歩圏。
deepdive.access_en: From Kintetsu Yamato-Yagi Station, take a Nara Kotsu bus toward Uda city (approximately 40 minutes). By car from Hari IC (Meihan Expressway), approximately 40 minutes. From Kintetsu Haibara Station, approximately 20 minutes by car toward Ohuda. Within walking distance of the Uda-ji Ohuda roadside station.
deepdive.photo_points_jp: かぎろひ現象の観測条件：①時期は旧暦11月17日（毎年12月中旬〜1月初旬ごろ・冬至直前〜直後）。②時間帯は日の出前の30〜60分（薄明の時間帯）。③方角は東の低空（阿騎野の高台から東方の山の端が見えること）。④気象条件：晴れた冷え込みの強い朝が必須（曇・雨天では発生しない）。「かぎろひの丘万葉公園」（阿紀神社近傍）が毎年かぎろひ観望会の会場となる（例年1月第2日曜日ごろ・要事前確認）。神社境内の能舞台と杉木立はどの季節でも撮影価値がある。
deepdive.photo_points_en: Kagirohi observation conditions: 1. Timing: around lunar November 17 (approximately mid-December to early January in the solar calendar; just before or after the winter solstice). 2. Time of day: 30–60 minutes before sunrise (civil twilight). 3. Direction: look eastward from elevated ground in the Akino Plain toward the mountain horizon. 4. Weather: clear, cold mornings are essential — the phenomenon does not appear on overcast days. The 'Kagirohi-no-Oka Manyoshu Park' adjacent to Aki Jinja hosts an annual kagirohi viewing event (usually the second Sunday of January — confirm in advance). The Noh stage and cedar grove are photogenic year-round.
deepdive.nearby_jp: かぎろひの丘万葉公園（徒歩圏）・道の駅「宇陀路大宇陀」・大宇陀地区の歴史的町並み・松山城跡・吉野葛の老舗。阿紀神社から龍鎮渓谷まで車で約30分。
deepdive.nearby_en: Kagirohi-no-Oka Manyoshu Park (walking distance); Uda-ji Ohuda roadside station; historic townscape of Ohuda district; Matsuyama Castle ruins; traditional Yoshino kudzu producers. Ryuchin Valley is approximately 30 minutes away by car.
deepdive.warnings_jp: かぎろひ現象の観望会開催日・時刻は年により変動する。参加の際は宇陀市観光協会または宇陀市文化財課に事前確認すること。早朝の防寒対策（12月〜1月は氷点下になる場合あり）が必須。
deepdive.warnings_en: The kagirohi viewing event date and time vary from year to year — always confirm with the Uda City Tourism Association or cultural affairs office before planning a visit. Heavy cold-weather clothing is essential for predawn December–January outings; sub-zero temperatures are possible.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 「かぎろひ」の現象については気象・大気光学的説明（曙光の散乱・陽炎状のミラージュ）と詩的表現（炎のように燃え立つ朝の光）の両方の解釈がある。現象の発生は気象条件に強く依存し・毎年必ずしも観察できるわけではない点に注意。「不確かな情報：」現地の能舞台の文化財指定区分については公開情報から確認できなかった。

summary_en: An Engishiki-listed shrine said to originate as the 'Uda no Agoki-miya' where Princess Yamato-hime enshrined Amaterasu Omikami during the reign of Emperor Suinin. The shrine stands in the Akino (Aki Fields) where the Man'yoshu poet Kakinomoto no Hitomaro composed the celebrated poem in the first scroll (no. 46): 'Higashi no no ni / kagiroi no tatsu miete / kaerimi sureba / tsuki katamukinu' ('In the eastern fields I see the shimmer of dawn's light rising; turning to look back, the moon has tilted'). The 'kagiroi' phenomenon — the shimmering of auroral light and heat-haze in the eastern sky before dawn around the winter solstice — is still observed, and an annual 'kagiroi-viewing gathering' is held in mid-December to mid-January. The precinct also contains a Noh stage built in the early Edo period (the 'Aki no Hotaru-Noh' is performed there each June).
highlights_en: Located in the Akino (Aki Fields) where Kakinomoto no Hitomaro composed Man'yoshu Vol. 1, No. 46 — 'In the eastern fields the shimmer of dawn's light (kagiroi) rises, and turning back, the moon has tilted'
The 'kagiroi' phenomenon: the shimmering auroral and heat-haze light appearing in the eastern sky before dawn in mid-winter (annual viewing gatherings held mid-December to mid-January)
An Engishiki-listed shrine, formerly a prefectural shrine (elevated 1902), associated with the tradition that Princess Yamato-hime enshrined Amaterasu Omikami here during the reign of Emperor Suinin
Deities enshrined: Amaterasu Okami, Akita-hime-no-Kami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, and Yagokoroomoikane-no-Kami (main hall), among others
An Edo-period Noh stage survives in the precincts; the 'Aki no Hotaru-Noh' performance is held there annually in mid-June

name: 龍鎮渓谷・龍鎮神社
name_kana: りゅうちんけいこく・りゅうちんじんじゃ
name_en: Ryuchin Gorge and Ryuchin Jinja Shrine
address: 奈良県宇陀市榛原荷阪
status: 現存（常時立入可・雨天・増水時は危険）
hours: 日中のみ推奨（夜間立入危険）
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約60分（名阪国道→国道165号→県道経由）
summary: 約1万5000年前の室生火山群の火山活動が生み出した深谷川上流の渓谷美。柱状節理の岩壁・エメラルドグリーンの滝壺・龍鎮神社の拝殿が一体となった秘境。室生赤目青山国定公園内。沢沿いのアクセスのため増水・落石・滑落に注意が必要。
highlights: コバルトブルーのエメラルドグリーン滝壺：室生火山群の火山岩が生み出した柱状節理の絶景
龍鎮神社：渓流の両岸に拝殿と祠が佇む神秘的な神社（龍神を祀る）
室生赤目青山国定公園内・自然保護指定エリア
近鉄室生口大野駅から徒歩圏（約40分）のハイキングコース
秋季の紅葉と清流の組み合わせが特に美しい

deepdive.history_jp: 龍鎮渓谷は深谷川（室生ダムに注ぐ）の上流に形成された渓谷。約1万5000年前の室生火山群の火山活動によって生み出された火山岩（凝灰岩・安山岩系）が侵食され・柱状節理の岩壁と碧い滝壺が形成された。龍鎮神社の歴史的詳細は公開情報からは確認できないが・渓谷の滝壺に龍神が宿るとする民間信仰が古くからあり・注連縄と祠が祀られている。室生龍穴神社の文脈と同様の水神・龍神信仰の系譜に位置づけられる。
deepdive.history_en: Ryuchin Valley was carved by the Fukaya River (which flows into Muro Dam) through volcanic rock deposited by the Muro volcanic group approximately 15,000 years ago. Erosion of these tuff and andesitic rocks produced the columnar-jointed cliff faces and vivid turquoise plunge pool. Detailed historical records of Ryuchin Shrine could not be confirmed from public sources; however, folk belief holds that a dragon deity dwells in the plunge pool, a tradition consistent with the broader water-deity/dragon-deity (Ryujin) worship found throughout the Uda region (cf. Muro Ryuketsu Shrine).
deepdive.religion_jp: 龍鎮神社は渓流の両岸に拝殿・祠が設置された小規模な神社で・龍神（水神）を祀る。遊泳・キャンプ・バーベキュー・花火の禁止は「神聖な龍鎮神社の域内」という宗教的理由による。室生赤目青山国定公園内でもあり・自然保護の観点からも制限されている。神社の祭神・創建・宗教法人としての登録状況は公開情報から確認できなかった（不確かな情報）。
deepdive.religion_en: Ryuchin Shrine is a small-scale shrine with a haiden (oratory) and small auxiliary shrines placed on both banks of the stream, enshrining a dragon deity (Ryujin/water god). The prohibitions on swimming, camping, barbecue, and fireworks are stated as respecting the shrine's sacred domain and also reflect Quasi-National Park regulations. The shrine's official enshrined deity, founding date, and religious corporation status could not be confirmed from publicly available sources (unverified information).
deepdive.architecture_jp: 龍鎮渓谷：深谷川上流の渓谷。龍鎮橋が渓谷への入口。遊歩道を進むと石の鳥居（龍鎮神社の入口）が現れる。滝壺の傍に龍鎮神社の小さな拝殿・祠が両岸に設置。周囲の岩壁は室生火山群の火山岩による柱状節理。陽光が当たるとコバルトブルー〜エメラルドグリーンに輝く滝壺が特徴。
deepdive.architecture_en: The gorge entrance is at Ryuchin Bridge over the Fukaya River. A stone torii (Ryuchin Shrine's gate) appears partway along the unpaved trail. The small shrine haiden and auxiliary shrines are placed on both banks at the plunge pool. The surrounding cliff faces display columnar jointing in volcanic rock from the Muro volcanic group. The plunge pool glows cobalt-blue to emerald-green under direct sunlight.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 室生赤目青山国定公園内（環境省指定）。龍鎮神社・龍鎮渓谷自体の文化財指定は確認されていない（不確かな情報）。渓谷を含む龍鎮エリアは奈良県の景観資産の記述（www.pref.nara.lg.jp）にも言及がある。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: The valley lies within the Muro-Akame-Aoyama Quasi-National Park (Ministry of the Environment designation). No specific cultural property designation for Ryuchin Shrine or the valley itself has been confirmed from public sources (unverified information). The area is referenced in Nara Prefecture's landscape asset registry.
deepdive.legends_jp: 渓流の滝壺に龍神が宿るとされる水神信仰が民間に伝わる。室生龍穴神社（吉祥龍穴）と同様の龍神崇拝の系譜にあり・宇陀市内では室生川流域・深谷川流域にまたがって龍神への信仰が連続している。龍鎮神社の「龍鎮」という名称自体が「龍が鎮座する・龍を鎮める」の意と考えられる。具体的な縁起伝承は確認できていない（不確かな情報）。
deepdive.legends_en: Folk belief attributes the plunge pool to the dwelling of a dragon deity, consistent with the dragon/water god worship tradition found throughout the Uda basin — notably at Muro Ryuketsu Shrine (Kissho Ryuketsu cave). The name 'Ryuchin' (龍鎮) can be read as 'the dragon settles here' or 'subduing the dragon,' though no specific founding legend or engi document has been confirmed from public sources (unverified).
deepdive.access_jp: 近鉄大阪線・室生口大野駅から深谷川沿いに徒歩約40分。または室生ダム管理事務所の駐車場（無料）に駐車後・徒歩約20分。龍鎮橋付近に指定駐車場はなく・近傍の路肩の広い箇所に駐車するか室生ダム管理事務所の駐車場を利用。国道165号線→室生ダム方面の道路経由。亀山ICから車で約60分。
deepdive.access_en: From Kintetsu Muro-guchi-Ono Station, walk approximately 40 minutes along the Fukaya River valley. Alternatively, park at the Muro Dam Management Office parking area (free) and walk approximately 20 minutes. There is no designated parking at Ryuchin Bridge — use wide roadside shoulders or the Muro Dam lot. Access from Route 165 toward Muro Dam. Approximately 60 minutes from Kameyama IC.
deepdive.photo_points_jp: ①滝壺とエメラルドグリーンの水面：晴天・日中（午前10時〜14時）に太陽光が谷底に差し込む時間帯が最良。②柱状節理の岩壁：谷底からのローアングルで垂直の岩柱群を捉える。③龍鎮神社の石鳥居と清流：鳥居と流れ・苔むした岩の組み合わせ。④秋の紅葉と渓流（例年10月下旬〜11月中旬が見頃）。偏光フィルター（CPLフィルター）があると水面の反射を抑えエメラルドグリーンが映えやすい。
deepdive.photo_points_en: 1. The plunge pool (emerald-green): best in clear weather when direct sunlight reaches the valley floor (approximately 10:00–14:00). 2. Columnar jointed cliff faces: shoot from a low angle at the base to capture the vertical rock columns. 3. The stone torii with stream and mossy rocks: a classic sacred-nature composition. 4. Autumn foliage above the gorge (usually late October to mid-November). A circular polarizing (CPL) filter suppresses surface reflections and intensifies the water color.
deepdive.nearby_jp: 室生龍穴神社・吉祥龍穴（車で約10分）・大野寺弥勒磨崖仏（車で約10分）・室生寺（車で約15分）・近鉄室生口大野駅（徒歩圏）・室生ダム管理事務所（徒歩起点）。
deepdive.nearby_en: Muro Ryuketsu Shrine and Kissho Ryuketsu (approximately 10 minutes by car); Ono-dera Maitreya relief (approximately 10 minutes by car); Muro-ji Temple (approximately 15 minutes by car); Kintetsu Muro-guchi-Ono Station (starting point for walking access); Muro Dam Management Office (trailhead for dam-side approach).
deepdive.warnings_jp: 沢沿いのルートのため岩が濡れていると非常に滑りやすい。増水時は立入危険（特に梅雨・台風・大雨後）。遊歩道の一部は舗装されておらず石がゴロゴロしている。ヘビが出ることがある（6〜9月特に注意）。遊泳・キャンプ・バーベキュー・花火は禁止（神域かつ国定公園内）。冬季（12〜2月）は凍結の恐れあり。露出の少ない服装・運動靴が必須。夜間立入は危険なため避けること。
deepdive.warnings_en: The streamside route becomes extremely slippery when rocks are wet. Entry is dangerous during or after flooding (especially during the rainy season, typhoons, and heavy rainfall events). Parts of the trail are unpaved with loose rocks. Snakes are occasionally encountered (June–September, exercise particular caution). Swimming, camping, barbecue, and fireworks are prohibited (sacred domain and Quasi-National Park regulations). Ice is possible in winter (December–February). Wear full-coverage clothing and sturdy walking shoes. Avoid nighttime entry.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 増水・落石・滑落注意：沢沿いのルートは雨天・増水時には非常に危険であり入山を控えること。龍鎮神社の詳細（祭神・創建・宗教法人登録等）については「不確かな情報」として公開情報から確認できなかった。渓谷内での遊泳・バーベキュー等は禁止されており違反すると国定公園法上の罰則対象になる可能性がある。駐車場が限定的なため繁忙期（秋の紅葉シーズン等）は渋滞・駐車困難が予想される。

summary_en: A gorge carved by the upper reaches of the Fukaya River, shaped by volcanic activity of the Muro volcanic group approximately 15,000 years ago. Columnar-jointed rock walls, an emerald-green plunge pool, and the sanctuary of Ryuchin Jinja Shrine together form a remote scenic precinct. The site lies within the Muroo-Akame-Aoyama Quasi-National Park. The gorge is accessible on foot from Kintetsu Muroguchi-Ono Station (approximately 40 minutes) and is particularly striking in autumn when the clear stream combines with foliage colour.
highlights_en: Cobalt-blue and emerald-green plunge pool: a geological formation produced by the columnar-jointed volcanic rock of the Muro volcanic group
Ryuchin Jinja Shrine: a sacred enclosure in which the shrine hall and a subsidiary shrine stand on either bank of the mountain stream, enshrining the dragon deity
Within the Muroo-Akame-Aoyama Quasi-National Park, a designated natural conservation area
A hiking course accessible on foot from Kintetsu Muroguchi-Ono Station (approximately 40 minutes)
The combination of autumn foliage and mountain stream is considered particularly beautiful in season

name: 須須神社・山伏山奥宮
name_kana: すずじんじゃ・やまぶしやまおくみや
name_en: Susu Jinja Shrine and the Yamabushiyama Oku-miya
address: 石川県珠洲市三崎町寺家4-2（本社）／石川県珠洲市狼煙町カ74（奥宮）
status: 現存（能登半島地震による被害あり・復興進行中）
fee: 参拝無料（宝物殿拝観料 大人300円・要予約）
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約3時間30分（北陸道経由・小松IC〜のと里山海道〜珠洲）
summary: 能登半島最北端に鎮座する延喜式内社。崇神天皇代の創建と伝わり・約1200年前に現在地へ遷座。国指定重要文化財の木造男神像と国指定天然記念物の社叢を有する。標高172mの山伏山山頂に奥宮が置かれ・義経ゆかりの「蝉折の笛」も収蔵する。2024年元旦の地震で甚大被害。
highlights: 国指定重要文化財「木造男神像（5体）」と国指定天然記念物「須須神社社叢」を保有
山伏山（標高172m）山頂の奥宮は式内社の本社とする説もある古代聖域
義経が海難救済の礼として奉納した「蝉折の笛」など義経伝承が残る
毎年3月15日に流鏑馬から派生した「的打神事」を斎行
2015年日本遺産「灯り舞う半島 能登 熱狂のキリコ祭り」の構成文化財

deepdive.history_jp: 第10代崇神天皇の御代（約2100年前）に能登半島最北端の山伏山（鈴ヶ嶽・標高172m）山頂に創建されたと伝わる。天平勝宝年間（749〜756年）に現在の寺家地へ遷座し・高座宮と金分宮の二社に分かれた。延喜式内社・県社として格式を有し・三代実録には貞観15年（873年）に従五位上の神階を賜ったと記される。室町時代には「三崎権現」「須須大明神」と称され・天正12年（1584年）加賀藩主前田利家が巡国の際に祈願所と定め・神田五町歩（石高75石）を寄進した。2015年には日本遺産構成文化財に認定された。
deepdive.history_en: According to tradition, Suzu Shrine was founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin (approximately 2,100 years ago) on the summit of Yamabushiyama (also known as Suzugadake, elevation 172m) at the northernmost tip of the Noto Peninsula. It relocated to the current site of Terake during the Tenpyo-Shoho era (749-756 CE), at which point it was divided into two shrines: Takakuragu and Kinbungu. Listed in the Engishiki, it received court rank in 873 CE. In 1584, Maeda Toshiie designated it a shrine for prayers and donated land endowments. It was recognized as a Japanese Heritage component in 2015.
deepdive.religion_jp: 主祭神は高倉彦神（たかくらひこのかみ・寺家高座宮）・木花咲耶姫命（金分宮）・美穂須々美命（みほすずみのみこと・奥宮）の三神。日本海側一帯の守護神・縁結びと生成化育・災難除けの御神徳で知られる。元禄10年（1697年）の記録によれば奥宮中腹に大燈明堂が設けられ・北海を渡る船を夜ごと護り・これが明治初年の禄剛崎灯台へ発展したと伝わる。珠洲一郡の総社として「お郡祭」「御出御幸」と称する郡内巡行祭儀が近年まで斎行されていた。
deepdive.religion_en: The main enshrined deities are Takakurahiko no Mikoto (Terake Takakuragu), Konohanasakuyahime no Mikoto (Kinbungu), and Mihosusumi no Mikoto (the inner sanctuary). Worshipped as the guardian of the Sea of Japan coast, the shrine's virtues encompass marriage, creation, and protection from calamity. Records from 1697 describe a great lantern hall at the mid-slope of the mountain, guiding ships through the night—a precursor to the Rokko-zaki Lighthouse built in the early Meiji era.
deepdive.architecture_jp: 本社は木造建築の高座宮と金分宮の二棟から構成される。国指定重要文化財の木造男神像5体が宝物殿に収蔵されており・うち最古のものは平安時代の作と推定される。奥宮は山伏山山頂に建つ小祠で・社叢（国指定天然記念物）はスダジイ・タブノキ・ヤブニッケイ・ヤブツバキ等が繁茂する北部照葉樹林の原生林（約1万坪・約250種の植物）であり・北方的限界の照葉樹林として学術的価値が高い。
deepdive.architecture_en: The main shrine consists of two halls, Takakuragu and Kinbungu. The treasury houses five wooden male deity statues designated as National Important Cultural Properties, the oldest dating to the Heian period. The inner sanctuary is a small shrine on the summit of Yamabushiyama, surrounded by a National Natural Monument grove—primary forest of Castanopsis sieboldii, Machilus thunbergii, and other warm-temperate broad-leaved trees, representing the northern limit of this forest type in Japan.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 国指定重要文化財：木造男神像（5体）。国指定天然記念物：須須神社社叢（北部照葉樹林）。奥宮の山伏山社叢は石川県指定天然記念物。承安5年（1175年）2月28日付の能登国司庁宣以下73点の所蔵文書は石川県指定文化財。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: Nationally designated Important Cultural Properties: five wooden male deity statues. National Natural Monument: the shrine grove (northern broad-leaved evergreen forest). The Yamabushiyama grove at the inner sanctuary is a Prefectural Natural Monument. A collection of 73 documents including the oldest one dated 1175 CE is designated a Prefectural Cultural Property.
deepdive.legends_jp: 源義経が文治2年（1186年）奥州平泉へ落ち延びる途上・海難に遭い須須神社に救われたという伝承が残る。感謝の礼として奉納したとされる「蝉折の笛」が宝物殿に収蔵されている。社名「須須」の語源については・須佐（スサ）神と関連する説・煤（すす）に由来する説・「清澄（すすむ）」に由来する説等が諸説あり定説はない。奥宮の山伏山は原始時代より北海航行の目標・漁だめの森として崇拝され・平安中期には烽火（狼煙）の中継地であったと伝わる。
deepdive.legends_en: Legend holds that Minamoto no Yoshitsune, fleeing to Oshu Hiraizumi in 1186, was saved from a shipwreck through the power of Suzu Shrine. The flute known as Semiorino-fue, said to have been offered in gratitude, is preserved in the treasury. The origin of the shrine name 'Suzu' remains disputed—theories variously connect it to Susa (the deity Susanoo), soot (susu), or the word for purity (susumu). Yamabushiyama has been venerated since antiquity as a seamark for voyagers and a sacred mountain.
deepdive.access_jp: 自動車：のと里山海道経由・珠洲三崎ICを出て能登さとうみ街道経由で約20分。公共交通：すずバス狼煙飯田（海）ルート「須須神社前」バス停より徒歩すぐ（1日数本のみ）。奥宮（山伏山）へは狼煙町側から徒歩約15分の山道が必要。駐車場あり（無料）。問い合わせ：須須神社 Tel 0768-88-2772。
deepdive.access_en: By car: via Noto Satomi Kaido from the Noto Sanrizuka IC on the Noto Satoyama Kaido, approximately 20 minutes. By public transport: Suzu Bus Noroshi-Iida (Sea) route, alight at 'Suzujinja-mae' stop. The inner sanctuary on Yamabushiyama requires a 15-minute hike from the Noroshi-cho side. Free parking available. Tel: 0768-88-2772.
deepdive.photo_points_jp: 鳥居前から日本海を望む構図が絶景。秋のキリコ祭り時期（9月）は大キリコを背景にした参拝景観が撮れる（拝観料200円追加で寺家の大キリコ見学可）。山伏山山頂の奥宮からは能登半島先端・禄剛崎灯台・立山連峰を遠望できる好展望地。早朝の海霧と社叢の組み合わせも魅力。
deepdive.photo_points_en: The composition looking out over the Sea of Japan from the torii gate is outstanding. During the autumn Kiriko festival (September), large kiriko lanterns make a dramatic backdrop. From the inner sanctuary summit, views extend to Cape Noroshi, Rokko-zaki Lighthouse, and the Tateyama mountain range. Morning sea fog drifting through the ancient grove creates an atmospheric scene.
deepdive.nearby_jp: 禄剛崎灯台（徒歩圏・日本列島のほぼ最先端）・道の駅狼煙（すぐ隣・海産物販売）・見附島（車で約30分）・珠洲道路。能登半島最先端の「聖域の岬」とも近接。
deepdive.nearby_en: Rokko-zaki Lighthouse (within walking distance, near the northernmost tip of the main islands), roadside station Noroshi (adjacent), Mitsukejima island (approximately 30 minutes by car). The 'Sacred Cape' at the northernmost point of Noto Peninsula is nearby.
deepdive.warnings_jp: 2024年1月1日の能登半島地震および同年9月の奥能登豪雨による被害が甚大。参拝前に最新情報を確認のこと。道路通行止め・仮設工事継続中の可能性あり。冬季は積雪・凍結による路面状況悪化に注意。奥宮への山道は整備状況を事前確認することを推奨。
deepdive.warnings_en: Severe damage occurred from the January 1, 2024 Noto Peninsula Earthquake and the September 2024 Oku-Noto heavy rains. Check current conditions before visiting. Road closures and ongoing construction work are possible. Winter driving hazards due to snow and ice. Confirm trail conditions before attempting the hike to the inner sanctuary.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 【能登半島地震被害・復興状況】2024年1月1日の能登半島地震により須須神社（本社：寺家高座宮・金分宮）は鳥居・狛犬・灯籠・社殿基礎に甚大な被害を受けた。奥宮（山伏山山頂）は拝殿がさらに傾き・本殿が倒壊・覆殿基礎まで破壊された。山麓の遥拝所は2024年8月に公費で解体。山頂の拝殿・本殿は倒壊危険のため2024年12月再建断念の決定がなされ・2025年2月にご神体は山頂本殿裏の仮祠へ遷座した。READYFORクラウドファンディング（目標400万円・ネクストゴール800万円）は2026年1月14日締切で支援総額841万円・383人が支援し目標を達成。珠洲市「地域コミュニティ施設等再建支援補助金」（補助率90%・上限3000万円）を活用した奥宮本殿・遥拝所再建プロジェクトが進行中（完了予定2028年3月）。また珠洲市（寺家）では約6000戸のうち全壊2500戸（40%）に及ぶ甚大な住家被害が発生しており・地域コミュニティ自体が復興過程にある。Instagram公式アカウント @suzu_jinjya にて復興情報を発信中。2026年時点で参拝は可能だが・施設整備は継続中のため事前確認を強く推奨する。

summary_en: An Engishiki-listed shrine enshrined at the northernmost tip of the Noto Peninsula, with a founding tradition in the reign of Emperor Sujin and a recorded relocation to the present site approximately 1,200 years ago. The shrine holds five wooden male-deity images designated National Important Cultural Properties and a shrine forest designated a National Natural Monument. The summit of Yamabushiyama (172 m above sea level), where the inner shrine stands, is also advanced by some scholars as the true hon-sha of the Engishiki-listed shrine — an ancient sacred precinct. The site retains traditions associated with Minamoto no Yoshitsune, including the 'Semiori-no-fue' (a flute said to have been dedicated by Yoshitsune as an offering of gratitude for rescue from a maritime disaster), and holds a ritual known as the 'Matachi Shinji' — a ceremony descended from mounted archery — each year on 15 March. The shrine is also a component of the Japan Heritage 'Light-Dancing Peninsula: Noto — The Ecstasy of the Kiriko Festival' (designated 2015).
highlights_en: National Important Cultural Properties: five wooden male-deity images; National Natural Monument: the Susu Jinja shrine forest
Yamabushiyama (172 m): the summit inner shrine, advanced by some scholarship as the original hon-sha of the Engishiki-registered Susu Jinja — an ancient sacred precinct
Yoshitsune traditions, including the 'Semiori-no-fue' flute — said to have been dedicated by Yoshitsune as thanks for rescue from a maritime disaster
The 'Matachi Shinji' ritual — descended from mounted archery (yabusame) — performed annually on 15 March
A component cultural property of the Japan Heritage designation 'Light-Dancing Peninsula: Noto — The Ecstasy of the Kiriko Festival' (2015)

name: 妙立寺（忍者寺）
name_kana: みょうりゅうじ（にんじゃでら）
address: 石川県金沢市野町1-2-12
status: 現存（現役寺院・完全予約制見学）
fee: 大人（中学生以上）1200円・小学生800円・団体・障がい者1000円。未就学児入館不可
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約2時間（北陸道小松IC〜金沢東IC〜金沢市内）
summary: 寛永20年（1643年）加賀藩3代藩主・前田利常が現地に移築した日蓮宗寺院。外観は2階建てに見えるが実際は4階建て7層・23部屋29の階段を有する迷宮構造。落とし穴の賽銭箱・隠し階段・金沢城への抜け穴とされる深さ25mの井戸など多数の仕掛けを備えた加賀藩の出城・砦とされる。
highlights: 4階建て7層・23部屋・29の階段という迷宮的建築構造（外観は2階建てに見える）
落とし穴の賽銭箱・切腹の間・隠し階段など幕府監視対策の仕掛けが随所に配置
深さ25mの巨大な戸室石の井戸（横穴を通じて金沢城まで続くとも伝わる）
明かり取り兼用の障子蹴込み階段（敵の足の影を映し槍で攻撃できる仕様）
1643年移築当時の建築を戦災・大火なく現代まで保持（江戸時代初期の緊張を体感）

deepdive.history_jp: 前身は慶長元年（1596年）に加賀藩初代藩主・前田利家が金沢城内に設けた祈願所。3代藩主前田利常が寛永20年（1643年）に現在の地（寺町寺院群内）へ移築建立。その後1659年に現在地へ再移転。1862年に祖師堂の一部が火災となったが直ちに復興。金沢は第二次世界大戦での空襲を免れたため・移築当初（1643年）の建築様式が現存する。徳川幕府との緊張状態が続く外様大名・前田家が万が一の幕府攻撃に備えて築いた出城・砦としての側面を持つ。「忍者寺」の通称は仕掛けの性質が忍者屋敷を連想させることに由来し・忍者の実在とは無関係。
deepdive.history_en: The precursor was a prayer hall established by the first Kaga Domain lord Maeda Toshiie in 1596. The third lord, Maeda Toshinaga, relocated it to the current site in the Teramachi Temple Quarter in 1643. Part of the preaching hall burned in 1862 but was promptly rebuilt. Kanazawa escaped wartime air raids, preserving the original 1643 structure. The temple functioned as a secret fortification for the Maeda clan, an outside domain under constant surveillance from the Tokugawa shogunate. The nickname 'Ninja Temple' derives from the building's resemblance to a ninja mansion—no actual ninjas were ever based here.
deepdive.religion_jp: 日蓮宗の寺院（山号：正久山）。本堂内陣には日蓮宗の本尊・大曼荼羅および鬼子母神等が祀られている。茶室「霞の間」には満月・朧雲・富士山をかたどった床の間が配され・天井が意図的に低く作られており（刀や槍を使えないようにする）・武の空間における精神的・美的要素が混在する。
deepdive.religion_en: A Nichiren Buddhist temple (mountain name: Shokyuzan). The main hall's inner sanctuary enshrines the Nichiren mandala and Kishimojin. The tearoom 'Kasumono-Ma' features decorative alcoves representing the full moon, hazy clouds, and Mount Fuji, with a deliberately low ceiling to prevent sword or spear use—reflecting the coexistence of martial and aesthetic elements.
deepdive.architecture_jp: 外観は2階建てに見えるが・内部は4階建て7層（中2階・中々2階という複数の中間層）で部屋数23・階段29か所。当時は3階建て以上の建築が禁じられていたため外観で層数を隠す必要があった。屋根突端の望楼にはかつてギヤマン（カラフルなガラス）が嵌め込まれ・遠く加賀平野を見渡す見張り台として機能した。明かり取り兼用の障子蹴込み階段・床板を持ち上げると現れる抜け道・落とし穴となる賽銭箱・5つの隠し扉がある「武者溜まりの部屋」（中2階）・深さ25mの戸室石くり抜き井戸（横穴から金沢城へ通じるとも伝わる）・富士山の絵が抜け穴になった床の間など多数の仕掛けが確認されている。図面は残存しておらず・いまだ未発見の仕掛けが存在する可能性もある。
deepdive.architecture_en: While appearing to be two stories from outside, the interior comprises four floors in seven layers (including multiple mezzanines), with 23 rooms and 29 staircases. Buildings over three stories were prohibited at the time, necessitating this disguise. A watchtower at the roof's peak once had panes of colorful glass (gyaman) for signaling across the Kaga Plain. Known mechanisms include: translucent shoji steps for detecting infiltrators by shadow, a lifting-floor escape route, a trapdoor offering box, a warrior staging room (mezzanine) with five hidden doors, a 25-meter-deep well carved from tomurostori stone (said to connect via tunnel to Kanazawa Castle), and a mural concealing an escape hatch. No blueprints survive, and undiscovered mechanisms may still exist.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 国指定・県指定等の文化財指定は現時点で確認できていない（不確かな情報）が・加賀藩時代の建築遺構として歴史的・建築史的価値が高い。金沢市の観光重要文化財施設として管理されている。寺町寺院群は国の重要伝統的建造物群保存地区に選定されており・妙立寺はその構成要素の一つ。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: No confirmed individual national or prefectural cultural property designation has been identified for the temple building (uncertain information). However, it holds high value as an Edo-period architectural artifact. The Teramachi Temple Quarter in which it is located has been designated a National Important Preservation District for Historic Buildings.
deepdive.legends_jp: 金沢城まで続く井戸の抜け穴は地元で語り継がれる伝承だが・実際に地下通路が確認されたという公式記録は見当たらず「不確かな情報」として扱う。忍者がこの寺を拠点にしていたという俗説も史実としての根拠はなく・あくまで建築の仕掛けが忍者屋敷を連想させるというのが命名の由来。
deepdive.legends_en: The legend of a tunnel connecting the well to Kanazawa Castle is a local tradition but has not been confirmed by any official record—treat as unverified information. The notion that actual ninjas were based at this temple is folklore without historical foundation; the 'ninja' nickname derives solely from the building's resemblance to a ninja hideout.
deepdive.access_jp: 完全予約制（電話予約のみ）。予約受付時間：8:30〜17:00。電話番号：076-241-0888（英語対応可）。拝観は約40分のガイド付きツアー形式（平日1時間毎・土日祝30分毎）。未就学児は安全上の理由から入館不可。小学校低学年は年齢証明（保険証等）の提示が必要。館内は撮影完全禁止。金沢駅からバス約15〜30分（広小路または野町広小路下車・徒歩2〜5分）またはタクシー約15分。駐車場なし（近隣有料駐車場利用）。
deepdive.access_en: Fully reservation-required, telephone reservations only. Phone: 076-241-0888 (English assistance available), reception hours 8:30–17:00. Tours are guided, approximately 40 minutes (hourly on weekdays, every 30 minutes on weekends and holidays). Children under school age are not admitted for safety reasons. Early primary-school children must show proof of age. Photography is strictly prohibited throughout. From Kanazawa Station: approximately 15–30 minutes by bus to Hirokoji or Nomachi-Hirokoji stops, then 2–5 minutes on foot, or 15 minutes by taxi. No dedicated parking (use nearby paid lots).
deepdive.photo_points_jp: 館内は撮影完全禁止のため・外観と寺町寺院群の街並みが唯一の撮影ポイント。犀川沿いの寺院群は外観スケッチや映え写真に向いており・秋の紅葉期は特に美しい。望楼（屋根頂部）の外観を外から見上げる構図も特徴的。
deepdive.photo_points_en: Photography inside the temple is strictly prohibited, so the exterior facade and the surrounding Teramachi temple streetscape are the only photography opportunities. The temple district along the Saigawa river is excellent for architectural shots, especially in autumn foliage. The distinctive watchtower projection at the roofline is a recognizable architectural feature.
deepdive.nearby_jp: にし茶屋街（徒歩圏）・犀川大橋・寺町寺院群（国の重要伝統的建造物群保存地区）・金沢21世紀美術館（タクシー約10分）・兼六園（タクシー約10分）。
deepdive.nearby_en: Nishi Chaya Geisha District (walking distance), Saigawa Ohashi bridge, Teramachi Temple Quarter (National Important Preservation District for Historic Buildings), 21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art Kanazawa (about 10 minutes by taxi), Kenroku-en Garden (about 10 minutes by taxi).
deepdive.warnings_jp: 完全予約制のため予約なしでの来館は不可。未就学児（概ね小学校入学前）は入館不可。小学校低学年は年齢証明書（保険証等）の提示が必要。館内は撮影完全禁止（スマートフォン含む）。予約時間に遅れた場合はキャンセル扱い・時間変更不可。冬期（12月〜2月頃）は受付終了時間が早まる場合があり要確認。
deepdive.warnings_en: Advance telephone reservation is mandatory; walk-in visits are not accepted. Children not yet in primary school are not admitted. Early primary-school children must present age proof. Photography of any kind (including smartphones) is strictly prohibited inside. Late arrivals are treated as cancellations and rescheduling is not possible. Closing hours may be earlier in winter (December–February); confirm when booking.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 拝観料は情報源によって1000円と1200円の二種類の記述が混在する。金沢市観光公式サイトおよび石川県観光公式サイト（2024年以降の情報）では大人1200円（小学生800円・団体等1000円）と記載されているため・現在の標準料金は1200円とみられる。古い情報（2019年以前）では1000円記載があるため注意。訪問前に寺院公式電話（076-241-0888）で最新料金を確認することを推奨。

summary_en: A Nichiren-sect temple relocated to its present site in the twentieth year of Kan'ei (1643) by Maeda Toshitsune, the third lord of the Kaga domain. Though the exterior presents the appearance of a two-storey building, the interior comprises four storeys and seven levels, with 23 rooms and 29 staircases — a labyrinthine architectural configuration. The structure incorporates numerous concealed defensive features devised to counter surveillance by the Edo shogunate: a pitfall-concealing offertory box, a ritual suicide chamber, hidden staircases, and a staircase with paper-screen risers designed to cast an enemy's shadow in silhouette for attack by lance. A well of cut Tomuro stone 25 metres deep — said by tradition to connect underground to Kanazawa Castle — is also incorporated. The building has survived to the present day without destruction by wartime bombing or major fire, preserving the architectural fabric of the early Edo period intact.
highlights_en: A four-storey, seven-level, 23-room, 29-staircase labyrinthine architectural structure — the exterior presents the appearance of a two-storey building
Concealed defensive features throughout: a pitfall offertory box, a ritual suicide chamber, hidden staircases, and paper-screen stair risers designed to silhouette intruders for lance attack
A Tomuro-stone well 25 metres deep — said by tradition to connect via a lateral passage to Kanazawa Castle
Illumination-admitting shoji-screen stair risers (designed to project the shadow of an enemy's foot for lance attack from below)
The 1643 relocated structure has survived to the present without wartime destruction or major fire — the architectural atmosphere of early Edo-period defensive anxiety remains palpable

name: 五箇山・塩硝の館
name_kana: ごかやま・えんしょうのやかた
address: 富山県南砺市菅沼134
status: 現存（2025年7〜8月に二階改修工事のため一時閉館期間あり）
fee: 五箇山民俗館との共通券：大人400円・小中学生200円（団体20名以上は大人300円・小中学生150円）
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約2時間30分（東海北陸自動車道経由・五箇山ICから車で約1分）
summary: ユネスコ世界遺産・菅沼合掌造り集落内に建つ展示施設。江戸時代に五箇山が加賀藩直轄の塩硝（硝酸カリウム）生産地として機能した歴史と製造工程を伝える。蚕の糞・人尿・草・土を合掌造りの床下で長期発酵させる「培養法」という独自のバイオテクノロジーを約300年間実施した。
highlights: 世界遺産・菅沼合掌造り集落内で実際の塩硝生産工程を学べる唯一の展示施設
蚕の糞・人尿・草・土を床下で5〜6年発酵させる「培養法」という当時最先端のバイオテクノロジー
加賀藩が約300年間藩直轄で秘密裏に管理した火薬原料生産の産業史
塩硝街道（五箇山〜金沢）という秘密輸送ルートとの関連
合掌造り集落の豪雪・隔絶性が産業秘密保持に利用された歴史的文脈

deepdive.history_jp: 五箇山での塩硝（硝酸カリウム・火薬の原料）生産は江戸時代（17世紀）から加賀藩の直轄事業として実施された。加賀百万石を誇る加賀藩は一国一城令のもとで幕府の警戒を受け・火薬の自国生産は軍事秘密として厳格に管理された。菅沼・相倉を含む五箇山の各集落は庄川沿いの深い谷に位置し・籠の渡しでのみ往来できる地の利が秘密保持に適していた。明治維新により加賀藩解体とともに生産は終了したが・技術は「培養法」として記録され現在の展示館で紹介されている。
deepdive.history_en: Saltpeter (potassium nitrate, a gunpowder component) production in Gokayama was a direct domain enterprise of the Kaga Domain from the 17th century. The Kaga Domain, one of Japan's most powerful, required gunpowder independence but had to conceal military production from Tokugawa scrutiny. Gokayama's deep valley settlements, accessible only by basket ferry, were ideal for maintaining secrecy. Production ended with the Meiji abolition of the domain, but the technique was recorded as the 'Baiyoho' (cultivation method) and is now presented in this museum.
deepdive.religion_jp: 塩硝生産は宗教的文脈とは直接関わらないが・五箇山は加賀藩の流刑地でもあり・浄土真宗（一向宗）信仰が強い地域であった。合掌造り家屋の大空間は塩硝を囲炉裏余熱で温めながら発酵させる場として機能した。
deepdive.religion_en: Saltpeter production had no direct religious context, though Gokayama was simultaneously a Kaga Domain exile site and a stronghold of Jodo Shinshu (Ikko-shu) Buddhism. The large thatched farmhouses served as fermentation chambers, warmed by the residual heat of central hearths.
deepdive.architecture_jp: 塩硝の館は菅沼集落の合掌造り家屋を展示施設として改修したもの。塩硝製造の「培養法」とは：囲炉裏の床下にすり鉢状の穴を掘り・土・蚕の糞・鶏糞・藁・枯草などを交互に積み重ねた後・人尿（おしっこ）を大量にかけて土をかぶせる→冬は囲炉裏の余熱で温めながら年1回程度混ぜ返して5〜6年発酵させる→微生物が尿素をアンモニア→亜硝酸→硝酸と変化させ「塩硝土」を生成→カリウムを含む灰汁と煮て「硝酸カリウム」（塩硝）を精製する。この方法は他藩の自然堆積硝酸塩採取法に比べて明らかに効率が高く・加賀藩の軍事力を支えた。展示には製造道具・工程図・資料が揃う。2025年7〜8月は二階改修工事のため一部閉館期間あり（富山県観光ナビ情報）。
deepdive.architecture_en: The Ensho no Yakata is a gassho-zukuri (steepled-roof) farmhouse adapted as an exhibition facility. The saltpeter 'cultivation method' (baiyoho): a mortar-shaped pit was dug beneath the hearth, layered alternately with soil, silkworm excrement, chicken dung, straw, and dry grass, then soaked with large quantities of human urine and covered with soil. Over five to six years, with annual turning and warming from the hearth's residual heat, microorganisms converted urea to ammonia, then nitrous acid, then nitric acid—producing saltpeter-rich soil. This was then boiled with potash-containing lye to extract potassium nitrate (saltpeter). This technique was markedly superior to other domains' methods of harvesting naturally occurring nitrates. The museum displays original tools, process diagrams, and documents.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 五箇山菅沼合掌造り集落は1995年にユネスコ世界文化遺産に登録（「白川郷・五箇山の合掌造り集落」）。塩硝の館はその集落内の施設として世界遺産バッファゾーン内に立地する。塩硝製造に関する文書・道具類は南砺市の文化財として保護されている。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: The Gokayama Suganuma Gassho-zukuri Village was inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site in 1995 (together with Shirakawa-go). The Ensho no Yakata is located within the heritage buffer zone. Documents and tools related to saltpeter production are protected as cultural assets of Nanto City.
deepdive.legends_jp: 五箇山から金沢へ塩硝を運ぶ山道は「塩硝街道」と呼ばれた。加賀藩はこの道を厳重に管理し・生産工程は藩の最高軍事機密の一つとして外部に漏れないよう徹底された。五箇山の人々は山間の陸の孤島という地理的条件を利用しながら・300年以上にわたり秘密生産を継続した。
deepdive.legends_en: The mountain path used to transport saltpeter from Gokayama to Kanazawa was called the 'Ensho Kaido' (Saltpeter Road). The Kaga Domain strictly controlled this route, and the production process was treated as one of the domain's highest military secrets. The people of Gokayama maintained this secret production for over 300 years, exploiting the geographic isolation of their mountain valley.
deepdive.access_jp: 東海北陸自動車道・五箇山ICから国道156号線を砺波方面へ約1分・菅沼展望広場駐車場へ（徒歩1分）。公共交通：JR城端線城端駅から世界遺産バスで約40分「菅沼」バス停下車・徒歩1分。電話：0763-67-3262。駐車場：菅沼展望広場駐車場（駐車協力金 2025年10月以降は普通車1000円）。
deepdive.access_en: By car: about 1 minute from Gokayama IC on the Tokai-Hokuriku Expressway along National Route 156 toward Tonami, then to Suganuma Viewing Area parking lot (1-minute walk). By public transport: World Heritage Bus from JR Johanashi Line Johana Station, approximately 40 minutes to 'Suganuma' stop, then 1-minute walk. Tel: 0763-67-3262. Parking: Suganuma Viewing Area lot (cooperation fee: 1,000 yen for standard vehicles from October 2025 onward).
deepdive.photo_points_jp: 菅沼展望広場駐車場の展望台から合掌造り集落全体を俯瞰する構図が最もインパクトがある。集落内の石畳と合掌造りの組み合わせは冬季積雪期（12月〜2月）に特に美しい。庄川と切り立った崖を背景にした集落景観も魅力。
deepdive.photo_points_en: The most dramatic composition is the bird's-eye view of the entire gassho-zukuri village from the viewing platform at the Suganuma Viewing Area parking lot. The combination of stone-paved paths and thatched roofs is especially beautiful in winter snowfall (December–February). The village framed by the Shokawa River gorge and steep cliffs is also photogenic.
deepdive.nearby_jp: 五箇山民俗館（共通券で見学可・すぐ隣）・菅沼合掌造り集落（世界遺産）・相倉合掌造り集落（車で約15分）・道の駅たいら（車で約15分）・白川郷（車で約20分）。
deepdive.nearby_en: Gokayama Folk Museum (shared admission ticket, immediately adjacent), Suganuma Gassho-zukuri Village (World Heritage Site), Ainokura Gassho-zukuri Village (approximately 15 minutes by car), roadside station Taira (approximately 15 minutes by car), Shirakawa-go (approximately 20 minutes by car).
deepdive.warnings_jp: 年末年始（12月29日〜1月1日）は休館。2025年7〜8月は二階改修工事のため臨時閉館期間あり（事前要確認）。冬季は積雪・路面凍結に注意。菅沼展望広場駐車場は2025年10月以降から普通車1000円に値上げ。
deepdive.warnings_en: Closed during New Year holidays (December 29 – January 1). Temporary closures occurred in July–August 2025 for second-floor renovation (confirm current status before visiting). Exercise caution driving in winter due to heavy snowfall and icy roads. The Suganuma Viewing Area parking fee increased to 1,000 yen per vehicle from October 2025.
deepdive.warnings_extra: 2025年7月〜8月に二階改修工事が実施されており・工事中は塩硝の館が閉館となる期間あり（富山県観光ナビ公式サイトにて確認）。この期間は五箇山民俗館のみ開館し・入館料が特別料金（大人300円）に変更された。2026年訪問時は最新の開館状況を必ず確認のこと。

summary_en: An exhibition facility within the UNESCO World Heritage Suganuma Gassho-zukuri village complex, dedicated to the history and manufacturing process of saltpeter (potassium nitrate) production in Gokayama during the Edo period, when the region operated as a directly administered production site of the Kaga domain. The production method — known as 'baiyoho' (cultivation method) — involved layering silkworm excrement, human urine, grasses, and soil beneath the floorboards and allowing the mixture to ferment for five to six years, representing a form of biological technology that was among the most advanced of its time. The domain maintained direct and secret control over this gunpowder-precursor industry for approximately 300 years, and the isolation and heavy snowfall of the Gokayama valley were themselves a structural element in maintaining industrial secrecy. The facility is the sole exhibition venue within the World Heritage village where the actual saltpeter production process can be studied.
highlights_en: The sole exhibition facility within the World Heritage Suganuma Gassho-zukuri village where actual saltpeter production processes can be studied

The 'baiyoho' (cultivation method): silkworm excrement, human urine, grasses, and soil fermented beneath floorboards for five to six years — a form of biological technology at the frontier of Edo-period knowledge
An industrial history in which the Kaga domain maintained direct and secret control over gunpowder-precursor production for approximately 300 years
The Ensho Kaido (Saltpeter Road, Gokayama to Kanazawa): the secret transport route for finished product
The heavy snowfall and geographical isolation of the Gokayama valley functioned structurally as a means of preserving industrial secrecy

name: 五箇山・流刑小屋
name_kana: ごかやま・るけいごや
address: 富山県南砺市田向（旧平村）
status: 現存（復元施設・外観のみ見学自由）
from_kameyama: 亀山ICから車で約2時間30分（東海北陸自動車道経由・五箇山ICから車で約10分）
summary: 江戸時代に加賀藩が設けた流刑地・五箇山の庄川右岸田向集落に現存する唯一の流刑小屋。重罪人を外出・交流禁止で幽閉した「お縮り小屋」の復元施設。原寸復元（間口2.6m・奥行3.6m）で食事の差し入れ口と格子戸を備える。昭和38年の豪雪で倒壊後・昭和40年に復元。富山県指定有形民俗文化財。
highlights: 全国唯一現存の流刑小屋（復元施設）・富山県指定有形民俗文化財
重罪人を完全幽閉した「お縮り小屋」の構造を原寸で再現（食事差し入れ口・格子戸）
加賀藩が庄川の籠の渡しを利用して脱獄を不可能にした流刑制度の体現
藩主への反逆・加賀騒動関係者・大槻伝蔵の孫など著名な政治犯が収監された歴史
江戸時代末期まで約200年間・150余名が五箇山に流刑となった「陸の孤島」の歴史

deepdive.history_jp: 五箇山が加賀藩の流刑地として正式に指定されたのは元禄3年（1690年）・高崎半九郎ら複数の藩士が遊女事件で摘発されたことを契機として・前田綱紀が「追放に適切な地」として選定した。流刑地は庄川右岸の7集落（猪谷・小原・田向・大島・篭度・大崩島・祖山）に限定され・明治元年（1868年）の大赦令まで約170年にわたり150余名の流人が送られた。田向集落への流刑は元禄3年（1690年）の安見与八郎が最初とされ・文久・嘉永年間まで続いた（集落文書に24名の記録）。最重罪の流刑人はこの「お縮り小屋（おしまりごや）」に幽閉された。加賀騒動で名高い大槻伝蔵の男子七之助（宝暦9年〜）もここに流された。現存する小屋は明和年間の大火後に新築されたものを昭和40年（1965年）に原寸復元したもので・食事差し入れ口の柱のみ倒壊前の材料が使用されている。
deepdive.history_en: Gokayama was officially designated as a Kaga Domain exile site following the 1690 investigation of a scandal involving retainers and courtesans; domain lord Maeda Tsunanori selected it as a suitable place for banishment. Seven villages on the right bank of the Shokawa River served as exile sites until the 1868 Meiji general amnesty—approximately 150 exiles over 170 years. The earliest exile to Tamukai village was Yasumi Yohachiro in 1690. The most severe offenders were confined in this 'oshimari goya' (locked hut). Otsuki Denso's son Shichino-suke, related to the infamous Kaga Scandal, was among those exiled here. The existing structure was rebuilt to original dimensions in 1965 after the original collapsed in the 1963 heavy snowfall (only the post with the meal-slot survives from the original).
deepdive.religion_jp: 流刑制度自体は宗教と直接の関連を持たないが・五箇山は浄土真宗（一向宗）信仰が強い地域であり・流人の中には藩の宗教政策に反した者もいた可能性がある。田向集落では流人が村人に学問を教えるなど一定の地域交流が許された平流刑人との交流も記録される。
deepdive.religion_en: The exile system had no direct religious connection, though Gokayama was a stronghold of Jodo Shinshu (Ikko-shu) Buddhism. Some exiles may have included those who defied the domain's religious policies. Records indicate that lower-status exiles (hira-goya inmates) were permitted limited community interaction, and some educated villagers.
deepdive.architecture_jp: 現存する流刑小屋は切妻造・妻入り・茅葺（現在は茅葺き様外観で保存）。規模は間口2.6m（九尺）・奥行3.6m（二間）・高さ約3m。外壁は耐久性の高い栗材を使用。内部は約6畳の板敷で・便所が付属する。柱には食事の差し入れ口（蓋付き穴）が設けられ・反対側には高さ1.2m・幅0.75mの格子の潜戸があり・流刑人の出し入れ時以外は大錠が懸けられた。流刑小屋には3種類あった：集落内を自由に出歩ける「平小屋」・外出不可の「お縮り小屋」・内部にさらに狭い檻のある「禁錮」。現存するのはお縮り小屋に該当する。本施設は「復元施設」であり・元の資材はほとんど残存していない点に注意。
deepdive.architecture_en: The surviving exile hut has a gabled roof construction and is maintained in the appearance of a thatched farmhouse. Dimensions are approximately 2.6m wide by 3.6m deep by 3m high. External walls use durable chestnut timber. The interior is a plank floor of about 6 tatami-mat equivalent, with a latrine. One post has a lidded slot for passing food; the opposite wall has a 1.2m × 0.75m latticed crawl-door locked except when inmates were admitted or removed. Three types of exile huts existed: the 'hira-goya' (freedom within the village), the 'oshimari-goya' (this building, total confinement), and the 'kinko' (a cage within the hut). This is a reconstructed facility; nearly all original materials have been replaced.
deepdive.cultural_property_jp: 富山県指定有形民俗文化財（昭和40年10月1日指定・当初は平村文化財・南砺市編入後に県指定）。全国で唯一現存する流刑小屋の遺構として民俗学上・歴史学上の価値が認められている。
deepdive.cultural_property_en: Designated as a Toyama Prefectural Tangible Folk Cultural Property (designated October 1, 1965, originally a Taira Village cultural asset, later upgraded to prefectural designation after merger into Nanto City). Recognized as the only surviving exile hut structure in Japan, with significant value in folklore and historical studies.
deepdive.legends_jp: 庄川に橋を架けることを加賀藩が禁じ・籠の渡し（藤づるで渡す一人乗りの籠）でのみ往来できる構造にしたことが逃亡を事実上不可能にした。この「籠の渡し」は塩硝の秘密生産を守る目的もあったと言われる。長年にわたって流人が農民に学問を教えた事例も残り・過酷な環境の中で独特の文化交流があったことが伝わる。
deepdive.legends_en: The Kaga Domain prohibited bridge construction over the Shokawa River, permitting only a basket ferry suspended on wisteria vines—making escape effectively impossible. This restriction is also said to have served to protect the secret saltpeter production. Records preserve cases of exiles teaching literacy and learning to villagers over the years, indicating a unique cultural exchange within harsh conditions.
deepdive.access_jp: 東海北陸自動車道・五箇山ICから車で約10分。JR城端線城端駅から加越能バス（五箇山・白川郷方面）で約32分・上梨バス停下車・徒歩約5分。田向集落は上梨集落の大平橋を渡った庄川右岸。見学自由・外観のみ。問い合わせ：五箇山総合案内所 Tel 0763-66-2468（9:00〜17:00）。
deepdive.access_en: Approximately 10 minutes by car from Gokayama IC on the Tokai-Hokuriku Expressway. Alternatively, from JR Johana Station take the Kaenoho Bus (Gokayama/Shirakawa-go direction) for about 32 minutes to Kaminashi stop, then 5 minutes on foot. Tamukai hamlet is across the Ohirabashi bridge over the Shokawa River from Kaminashi. Open for exterior viewing at all times. Inquiries: Gokayama General Information Center, Tel: 0763-66-2468 (9:00–17:00).
deepdive.photo_points_jp: 茅葺き外観と背後の切り立った庄川の崖が重なる構図が印象的。周囲の合掌造り集落（田向集落）と合わせて江戸時代の隔絶感を感じさせる場所。冬季積雪期は特に孤絶した雰囲気が増す。
deepdive.photo_points_en: The composition pairing the thatched-roof exterior with the sheer Shokawa River cliffs behind is striking. Combined with the surrounding Tamukai hamlet farmhouses, it evokes the isolation of the Edo period exile experience. Winter snowfall greatly intensifies the sense of remoteness.
deepdive.nearby_jp: 五箇山総合案内所（上梨・徒歩5分）・国指定重要文化財・村上家（上梨・徒歩3分）・菅沼合掌造り集落（車で約10分）・相倉合掌造り集落（車で約5分）。
deepdive.nearby_en: Gokayama General Information Center (Kaminashi, 5 minutes on foot), nationally Important Cultural Property Murakami-ke farmhouse (Kaminashi, 3 minutes on foot), Suganuma Gassho-zukuri Village (about 10 minutes by car), Ainokura Gassho-zukuri Village (about 5 minutes by car).
deepdive.warnings_jp: 本施設は「復元施設」であり・昭和40年（1965年）に古文書の記録に基づいて原寸で復元したもの。元の建築材料はほとんど残存していない。外観のみの見学で内部には入れない場合あり（現地看板確認要）。冬季は積雪・路面凍結に注意。五箇山ICから田向集落への道路は狭路のため注意が必要。
deepdive.warnings_en: This is a reconstructed facility, rebuilt to original dimensions in 1965 based on historical documents. Almost none of the original building materials survive.
